cq_num: Early rising
text: Early rising means getting up from bed very early in the morning. Undoubtedly, its a very good habit and it benefits us in many ways. An early riser, whether a student or in any profession or career can say his morning prayers and can enjoy a morning walk or physical exercise in the fresh air of the dawn which ensures fitness and happiness of his body and mind. With a vigorous mind, an early riser can think upon the creation of the universe in a very peaceful and sound environment and thus, he is spiritually elevated. By the early start, he can work steadily and finish timely and properly. And hence, he becomes either a punctual student or a professional and an active person as well. In addition, he becomes a resourceful person in all respects, for example, knowledge, money, power, job, prestige which makes him a solvent person in the long run. On the other hand, without early rising one can achieve nothing of the above advantages and is deprived of a lot of wellbeing and always lags behind in all corners such as: physically, mentally, spiritually and financially as well. Finally we can recollect the famous universal maxim that: "Early to bed and early to rise, makes a man healthy, wealthy and wise.

cq_num: A good teacher/ an ideal teacher
text: A good teacher/an ideal teacher is one of the most important persons in a country. Needless to say that a teacher is an architect of a nation. But to be a good teacher, one has to have excellent academic qualification and proper training as well. Teaching is not only a profession but also a mission. So, one must be determined to accept teaching as both a profession and a mission of life. Simultaneously, one should have some noticeable qualities or has to acquire these qualities. He/she will be an eloquent speaker or clear speaker with pleasing voice to attract his/her students for better and effective teaching. He/she will not stand or sit still before the students all the while. Sometimes he/she will be like an actor showing facial expression, gesture and physical movement to impress the students. Every student has some latent talent or hidden treasure. A good teacher must be able to explore the talent inside a student by his/her prompt wit to make them confident about their abilities and skills. He/she has to elicit information, ideas, opinions, views to develop their inner qualities in full. He/she must have a smiling and impressing face to make his students happy and jolly He/she of course will keep them busy in work. He/she should not hanker after money or riches but well being and the greater interest of the society or nation. Out of his responsibility, he/she has the noble duty to build a self reliant nation above all.

cq_num: Tree Plantation
text: Tree plantation means planting or growing trees in large number. Tree plantation is the crying need of the day. Trees are a great boon to us from god. They are our best friends. We cannot think of our existence on the earth without trees. Tress are very important and helpful to us in many ways. They are also a great source of food, money, comfort, protection and what not. The trees keep the ecological balance in the environment between human beings, animals and plants. Reckless cutting down of trees and deforestation are harming our climate and ecosystem tremendously. The world's temperatures are getting warmer gradually. If trees aren't planted, we won't get food, oxygen, timber, wood, vitamins and furniture herbal medicines, and many other different kinds of needful things for the want of the trees. So, there is a close relationship between trees and humans. If there are no trees, the environment will surely lose its ecological balance. Our beloved earth will be seriously affected by the much talked Green House Effect. Many parts of the human habitat or living places will be submerged into water or go under water. In a word, there would be no existence of living being in the world. It is a matter of great sorrow that we are indiscriminately destroying our tress which has now become a threat to the ecological balance. So, for the existence of mankind, we should be conscious about the importance of planting trees. We all should raise the the awareness of the people about the tree plantation and we have to make them understand that trees are our great friend. There are many places for planting trees. As there is no particular time or place for planting trees, so we can plant trees round the year anywhere. But specially June and July are the best time for tree plantation. During this period we can go for the tree plantation program together with our neighbors and can raise Massive awareness among people for tree plantation. We can plant trees in our garden, school campus, on river sides, on the roads sides, sea beaches and on low-lying unused lands or fallow lands even any suitable place for a better habitat for the greater interest of the mankind.

cq_num: Food Adulteration
text: Food adulteration has become a serious problem in recent years, especially in developing countries like ours. It refers to the practice of mixing or adding harmful, inferior, or toxic substances to food items in order to increase profit. Unscrupulous traders and dishonest businessmen use this method to cheat consumers and maximize their earnings, but in doing so, they endanger human health and society as a whole. Common examples of adulteration include mixing water with milk, adding soap or chemicals to sweets for better color and texture, using formalin to preserve fish and fruits, mixing brick dust with chili powder, and applying artificial colors to vegetables and confectionery items. These harmful chemicals and substances not only reduce the nutritional value of food but also cause severe diseases like diarrhea, kidney failure, cancer, high blood pressure, and even death. The situation worsens because consumers are often unaware of such adulteration and purchase these foods unknowingly. Adulterated food also weakens the next generation by damaging children’s growth and immunity. To fight against this evil practice, the government must enforce strict laws and punish those who engage in food adulteration. Modern testing facilities should be made available to detect unsafe foods in markets. At the same time, people must remain alert and conscious while buying food items. Public awareness campaigns, social responsibility, and ethical business practices can play a vital role in eradicating this problem. If we fail to address the issue immediately, food adulteration will create a health catastrophe that threatens the progress of our nation. Therefore, it is our moral duty to resist this menace and ensure safe, pure, and nutritious food for all.

cq_num: Deforestation
text: Deforestation is the process of cutting down of trees at a large scale or indiscriminately. The word deforestation is the opposite to afforestation. Deforestation poses a serious threat to the existence of all the living beings mankind, animals and plants. Tress keep the ecological balance in the environment. If trees are insufficient or no trees, our beloved mother earth will lose her ecological balance and beauty which gradually and finally leads to the loss of her existence. To keep an ecological balance in the nature 4 portion of the total land area of the earth should have forest. Human beings inhale oxygen and exhale carbon oxide in breathing. On the other hand, the trees take carbon dioxide and produces oxygen in the atmosphere which keeps a balance for living. But due to deforestation the earth is going to be a threatened, dangerous and a risky zone for human habitat. For deforestation or for the lack of afforestation, different sorts of natural calamities like global warming, climate changes, drought or heavy downpour, prolong flood or water logging, the green house effect have been escalated to a great extent. So, to save our mother earth for us and for the future generation, in no time we have to plant saplings, seedlings and Trees more and more at any suitable piece of land. To save us from the grip of deforestation, we have to intensify and accelerate, afforestation program --a revolutionary change must be brought in every place across the whole world. To impart education, the adverse effect of deforestation must be the integral part of our academic curriculum. We have to chant Sologan.” our stand is against deforestation and trees are our lives, they bring us food and good, so plant trees as much as you could.”

cq_num: Global Warming
text: Global Warming is a matter of serious concern for our mother earth. Climate change is fully responsible for it. And behind this degradation of the nature, humans and their machines are mostly liable. In between recent few decades, the temperature is climbing alarmingly and faster than any time of the past. Due to global warming, the ice caps of the South and North poles are melting fast and many countries are already experiencing adverse impacts of it. Among the other adverse impacts, irregular monsoon,heavy rainfall causing prolong water logging, landslide and as well as salinity of water as well as river erosion, drought, cold spell, and so on. Besides, as the globe warms up, the weather gets more violent and extreme and causes frequent storm and cyclone. If it continues, at a stage, many parts of our beloved earth belonging to some of the best land for growing food will be overflooded and the flood water will swallow the huge area of human habitat. Even some big cities like, London, kolkata, Bangkok and many other big cities will be submerged. In a word, if the global warming mounts such rapidly the mother earth will lose its ecological balance and human habitat will be threatened i.e, the existence of all living being will be at high risk and in great danger. To address this burning issue and to keep our next generation safe and sound, we all have to use environment -friendly machineries and to plant more and more tress to keep the plannet green and carbon dioxide free.

cq_num: A street accident
text: The invention of a motorized vehicle is both a boon and blessing of modern science but sometimes it's a curse when an accident happens and brings severe casualties' to human lives. However, nothing is without unmixed blessings in this world. In fact, the road communication in modern era has added a new dimension to the human lives and civilization but the means of this fast communication, streets are not free from accidents. Usually it happens in the highways where motorized vehicles run at a great speed in fast driving lanes and routes, or in the busy turning points, or in the zigzag road. There are many causes behind a street accident. The most important reasons of them are: defective vehicles and roads, lack of awareness of the drivers, long time on long drive without proper rest, lack of sufficient training, very fast speed of the vehicles, zigzag road, pot holes, lack of underpasses or foot over bridges, unawareness of the traffic rules, lack of traffic lights at the important crossing points, lack of zebra crossing for the passers-by, unlicensed drivers etc, However, a street accident may not be only a very trifle matter or the talking topic. An accident not only is a big loss, pains and agony of the near and dear ones to the bereaved family, but it is surely very painful, irreparable loss and great shock to the society or nation in different respects. So, to address this grave issue, the concerned authority and the conscious citizen of the country have to take collaborative measures and action to uproot the causes of the street accidents.

cq_num: Traffic jam
text: Traffic means vehicles and jam stands for congestion or blocking or clogging. So, Traffic jam is a long line of vehicles on a road that cannot move or that can only move very slowly. It is the common phenomenon in busy and mega cities. There are so many reasons behind traffic jam. Mainly, narrower roads, defective and slow speedy vehicles like rickshaws and push-carts or motorless vehicles, unpermitted road crossing by passers-by and parking and turning, flaws in traffic rules and management system or mismanagement of the authority, loading and unloading goods and passengers here and there, are responsible for traffic jam. Besides, poor condition of the roads, flooding, unplanned and uncoordinated digging of the different authorities like RHD, WASA,TNT and Electric Supply Authority also cause traffic jam a lot. On working days mostly in pick hours roads are packed with the traffic jam and precious time is killed or lost in vain. Due to traffic jam office going people are late. Students can't attend classes or take part in exams timely. Ambulances carrying dying/serious patients can't reach hospitals in time for better or follow up treatment and fire brigades can't reach the spot to extinguish the fire in time. Even, it happens sometimes that patients meet their immature deaths on the way. To eliminate traffic jam, spacious roads, more fly-overs, well planned Udner-Passes and Foot-Over-Bridges should be built. On the other hand, speed- wise seperate lanes, strictness in traffic rules, modernisation of traffic systems can address the issue of traffic jam to this end.

cq_num: A Rickshaw puller
text: person who pulls a rickshaw in order to earn his living is called a rickshaw puller. He is a very familiar figure across the country. In rural areas he is a shithole dweller or lives in a shabby hut or in the towns or the cities he lives in a slum. Usually his dress is torn and has no rickshaw of his own. He has to pull his rickshaw in both fine and rough weather to earn a square meal. Generally, a rickshaw puller living in a mega city earns more than his counterpart living in the rural areas. As rickshaw fare has no hard and fast rule. So, the fare is settled between the rickshaw puller and the passenger instantly by mutual negotiation. Nowadays rickshaw fare is very rampant. It's totally beyond the means of low-earning common mass. However, a rickshaw puller's life is full of sorrows and sufferings. When he suffers from a malady or an illness he cannot pull his rickshaw and cannot earn money for living. On the consequence, he and his family have to starve. But the day he earns more his face beams with joy. Really, his hope and expectation is not so lofty or sky scrapping or sky touching. He is contended whatever he earns. Though he cannot eat very well with his scanty income he can sleep very well. To conclude, a rickshaw puller keeps an important role in the communication of short distance and for the low and middle income people. In Bangladesh, on strike or hartal days a rickshaw is the only means of transport to ease our communication.

cq_num: Our School Library
text: A library is an abode of gaining knowledge and information. It is a place where a huge collection of books, newspapers. journals, magazines as well as CDs, DVDs for electronic device on different branches of knowledge are kept for learners to read, study, or for research work. Every renowned school has a library. so, do we. There are two sections in our library: one is borrowing section and another is reference section. One student can use these resource items in the library or one can borrow these according to his/her need. But one cannot borrow anything from the reference section. It satisfies our eternal thirst for learning. Indeed, a library is an integral part of a school. No school is thorough without a library. Ours is a very big modern library in the first floor of the main school building. It is fully equipped with all the modern facilities such as modern furniture, fully air conditioned, access to online etc. our school library runs under a trained up librarian and her two assistants. About 100 students can read here at a time. A huge collection of books amounting more than 10,000 copies are available in our school library. Every year some new and latest books are bought and procured from various sources so that a student can keep abreast of all the latest and update information. Here all the academic and reference books are arranged into classes or groups and then sorted alphabetically for the quick access to the particular or desired books from the prolific collection of books. Here, learners have to have membership cards for both reading and borrowing books. Books are issued with the library cards very promptly and easily with the assistance of the catalogue. Borrowing books are validated only for two weeks. Not more than 2 books can be issued against a membership at a time. For the smooth function of the library, sometimes students are penalized for the delay return of the borrowing books. Our library opens at 9 a.m. and closes at 4 p.m except holidays. The maxim' Silence is golden' is strictly in force in our library. I am really ecstatic and feel proud of having such an enriched library.

cq_num: Our National Flag
text: Every independent nation of the world has a national flag. So, do we. A national flag is the pictogram or symbol of a country's independence, sovereignty and her identity of being an independent state. Our national flag is our pride and our entity on the world's map. Very few nations of the world had to fight and sacrifice their countrymen's invaluable lives and blood to achieve its independence and to get its national flag. Hence, we have a different story to get it. Our glorious national flag is purchased at a great price i.e. by dint of millions people's blood. Painter Quamrul Hasan is the designer of our national flag. It is rectangular in shape. It has two colors: red in the centre and green around the center. Its centre looks like a rising sun on the background of green field. It has different sizes for different purposes but it will be made in proportion to 10:6 i.e. if the length is 10 feet, the width is 6 feet or if the length is 10 inches, the width is 6 inches. Here, the rising sun or the red disc represents the supreme sacrifice and courage of our people of the country and the green color symbolizes the prosperity, greenery, vitality and youthfulness etc. As a whole, our national flag emblematizes the sovereignty and the unity of Bangladesh. The national flag is hoisted atop in the Government, semi-government, autonomous and non-government any other organizations but it is kept lowered or hoisted at half ma non-government en bassies or mast on national mourning day or any other mourning days celebrated nationally. Our heart swells up with joy when we see our national flugs flying. In a word, it is our pride, spirit, hope, inspiration and more. So, to uphold its value and significance, we all must keep our national flag at its sublime position at any cost at any moment.

Hence, we have a different story to get it. Our glorious national flag is purchased at a great price i.e. by dint of millions people's blood. Painter Quamrul Hasan is the designer of our national flag. It is rectangular in shape. It has two colors: red in the centre and green around the center. Its centre looks like a rising sun on the background of green field. It has different sizes for different purposes but it will be made in proportion to 10:6 i.e. if the length is 10 feet, the width is 6 feet or if the length is 10 inches, the width is 6 inches. Here, the rising sun or the red disc represents the supreme sacrifice and courage of our people of the country and the green color symbolizes the prosperity, greenery, vitality and youthfulness etc. As a whole, our national flag emblematizes the sovereignty and the unity of Bangladesh. The national flag is hoisted atop in the Government, semi-government, autonomous and non-government any other organizations but it is kept lowered or hoisted at half ma non-government en bassies or mast on national mourning day or any other mourning days celebrated nationally. Our heart swells up with joy when we see our national flugs flying. In a word, it is our pride, spirit, hope, inspiration and more. So, to uphold its value and significance, we all must keep our national flag at its sublime position at any cost at any moment.

cq_num: The life of a farmer
text: A farmer is a person who lives on farming, i.e., grows crops to earn his living. He usually lives in the rural areas so that he can have easy access to wide open fields and plains where he can cultivate. He lives from hand to mouth. Не leads a rather miserable life by no means a luxurious one. He lives in a simple homestead-not so well furnished with modern amenities. He toils hard day and night in his fields to grow more and more food to feed the whole nation. He works from dawn to dust. He nurtures the tender plants like his own kids. He takes care of his crops as much as he can. When his crops grow in the fullest scale, he realizes his hard work has paid off. But his hard labour seldom pays off. Most often he is cheated from his rightful earnings. He is never respected but always downtrodden in our society. He is considered a worthless or illiterate. His profession is not recognised as a prestigious career in our society. Rather he is looked down upon and weighed down in the society. But a farmer is he who feeds us- the entire nation with his gracious labor. We cannot survive if the farmers stop farming. His valour and chivalrous labuor towards cultivation guarantee us the adequate food. Really he works for nourishing us; to keep the whole nation healthy, wealthy and free from starvation. His debt can never be repaid. We are indebted to him. So, a farmer is the golden son of any nation.

cq_num: A winter morning
text: A winter morning may be cold and foggy but very pleasant and comfortable while basking in the sun and lounging under the quilt. Everything seems hazy and dim. The grass remains wet with dew drops. When the sun rises, dew drops sparkle like pearls and wither soon. Old people and children shiver in cold. Poor people gather straw and make fire to bask in the heat. Animals, too are helpless. They hide themselves in the corner of the houses and try to save themselves from the biting cold outside. And the wild animals stay at their respective cavers and the birds stay at their nests. People get up late in the morning. Old people and children get up a bit later to evade cold of the early morning. Working people get out of the doors when the fog disappears and when the sun is hot. In a winter morning, people usually eat homemade cakes and piayas with 'date juice' is the sun or sitting outside their homes to enjoy the warmth of the early sun. Bangladeshi migrants settled across the globe come to enjoy and take the taste of winter morning in Bangladesh. They find the winter morning here pleasant and enjoyable as the winter in any European country is tremendously bitter and cold. A winter morning in Bangladesh is thus quite fascinating for them. Sometimes, the fog is so dense that the sunrays cannot get through it. Everything looks hazy and vague. As a result, communication system is disrupted. On the other hand, a winter morning is a little bit bane for the poor but a boon for the rich

cq_num: Climate Change
text: Climate change is the most pressing/talked about issue at the moment over the world. The cause of climate change can be divided into two categories those are due to natural causes and those are created by men. Global worming is the main natural reason and using harmful chemicals in every day life the main reason created by man. The climate is changing rapidly that result in the rise of the earth's average temperature. The global climate change and its consequences are leaving a bad impact on the developing countries to face natural calamities and poverty. Climate change impacts include temperature rise, greenhouse and carbon dioxide gas emissions, erratic rainfall, salinity intrusion, rise of floods, cyclones, storm surges and draught, ice sheets melting which will seriously affect the agriculture and livelihood, especially at the poor. Bangladesh, for its geographical locations, is likely to be the most affected. The developed nations, which are more responsible for such climate changes, should take responsibilities to protect the victimized countries. The Maldives is also one of the worst victims of climate change. A one meter sea-level rise will submerge about one-third of the total area of Bangladesh, which will uproot 25-30 million people of Bangladesh. These people will become refuges of climate change. To reduce the bad impact of climate change people should be aware. Tree plantation can reduce global warming which is the main natural reason of climate change. Stop using harmful chemicals can reduce environmental pollution which is the main man made reason of climate change. Students, environmentalists and all other concerned should be careful to protect the environment and raise awareness and can play a vital role to reduce bad/adverse impact of climate change.

cq_num: A Rainy Day
text: A rainy day is always special and full of beauty. When dark clouds gather in the sky and cool wind begins to blow, nature looks calm and peaceful. Suddenly, the rain starts falling in tiny drops and then turns into heavy showers. Gradually, the rain becomes heavier, and the sound of raindrops on tin roofs, trees, and roads creates a pleasant music. The whole environment turns cool and calm. Nature seems to smile under the refreshing touch of rain.On a rainy day, the roads become wet and sometimes muddy. Water gathers in low-lying areas, and people move carefully with umbrellas or raincoats. In cities, traffic jams often occur due to waterlogging. However, children feel very happy. They love to play in the rain, float paper boats in small streams, and jump into puddles of water. In villages, farmers welcome the rain because it is a blessing for their crops. The fields look greener and fresher after rainfall. The rain washes away dust and pollution, making everything look clean and bright. A rainy day also brings a special feeling of comfort. Many people prefer to stay at home, enjoy hot tea, and eat delicious snacks while watching the rain from their windows. Sometimes schools and offices remain closed due to heavy rainfall, which adds extra joy for students. However, poor people who live in fragile houses suffer a lot during continuous rain. Floods may damage crops and homes. In spite of some difficulties, a rainy day brings peace, freshness, and new life to nature. It refreshes our mind and fills our heart with joy. Truly, a rainy day is a beautiful gift of nature that adds charm to our everyday life.

cq_num: A tea stall
text: A tea stall is a kind of shop where tea as well as biscuits, snacks, beetle leaves are sold. It is usually seen at bus stops, rail stations, Water Transport Terminal, inside the airports, Ferry Ghat, university or college campus, close reach of the offices or any suitable public places where mass people throng to have a cup of tea. Tea is served hot but in some exclusive and expensive restaurant ice tea is also served. Though a tea stall has no regular or binding time table. It generally opens early in the morning and closes at midnight. As a whole, all walks of people come to a tea stall except some reserved and VIPs. If it is a foggy winter morning, the poor and the common mass seem crazy to sip over the cup of hot tea as a warm up session of the day. Most of the time of the day, a tea stall is found busy and lively with the discussion of the recent key and touching topics of the politics or important social, national or global issues. And, sometimes, it becomes the mini- parliament of the mass people. Through the discussion mass people can be aware of their duties and responsibilities, right and wrong. They can build up awareness among themselves. Thus, a tea stall keeps a vital role to make the people aware of the growing concern of the economical, social or political related matters.

cq_num: A book fair
text: A book fair is a wonderful event where publishers, booksellers, writers, and readers come together to celebrate the world of books. It is usually held in a large open space or exhibition hall and attracts people of all ages. The main purpose of a book fair is to promote reading habits and introduce people to new books, authors, and ideas. Stalls are set up by different publishers displaying books on various subjects such as literature, science, history, religion, art, and children’s stories. People can browse through the collections, read summaries, and buy books at reasonable prices. Many book fairs also organize special events like book signings, discussions, storytelling sessions, and cultural programs, which make the atmosphere lively and engaging. In Bangladesh, the most famous book fair is the Ekushey Boi Mela, held every February to honor the martyrs of the Language Movement. This fair not only sells books but also preserves our culture, language, and history. A book fair inspires people to read more and develop their knowledge. It is also a meeting place for authors and readers to share ideas and experiences. Visiting a book fair is both enjoyable and educational because it opens the door to the vast world of literature. It teaches us that books are our true friends, guiding us toward wisdom, imagination, and enlightenment. In today’s digital age, a book fair reminds us of the irreplaceable joy of holding and reading a printed book. Therefore, book fairs should be organized regularly to encourage reading and keep the love of books alive in society.

cq_num: My visit to the book fair
text: A book fair is the exhibition of different kinds of books. It is a place where various kinds of books are displayed for both sale and publicity. People from all walks of life specially the literates and intellectuals throng here. The teens visit the book fair to find their favourite books. However, it is considered an occasion of great festivity to the book lovers. It is usually held in winter in an open place. A huge number of book fairs are held across the country. Among all, the Ekushey Boimela is prominent and noteworthy. Last year, I paid a visit to the Ekushey Boimela. When I heard the opening of the fair, I along with some of my class mates talked to our class teacher and expressed our intention to visit this mega event. After consulting the principal, he agreed to accompany us to the Ekushey Boimela. On the very day, we hired an air-conditioned bus and went to visit this grand fair. We started for the fair around 11 o'clock. We were stunned to watch this great event. A festive look prevailed all around. There was eye catching collections of books everywhere. Security arrangements were very tight and strict. We had to pass through security gate after having being checked by the metal detector. There were so many decorated stalls organized by the publishers. The stalls dealing in stationery and children's books were very impressing. A huge crowd thronged over them. Each publisher had his own way to attract the public either by offering discount or by gift scheme. Some publishers had made special sitting arrangement. We saw some writers busy with their fans to put autographs. I bought a book of my own choice- a book of Shakespeare. But, alas! How time flies on. It was almost 5 o'clock. Our teacher told us to pack up and we all approached towards the gate to avail of the return journey. Really it was an awesome and memorable trip for us.

cq_num: Price Hike
text: Price hike is the sudden increase in prices or costs of commodities. Price hike is a serious bane on the economy of Bangladesh. The vast majority of the consumers are hard hit by the recent hike in prices of the daily necessities. Price hike is caused by short supply of commodities and inflation. The price hike that results from short supply of commodities is temporary. The prices come down if their supply is enhanced. But the price hike caused by inflation cannot be controlled by easy efforts. Market syndicate is mostly responsible for price hike in Bangladesh. Moreover, some dishonest traders create artificial crisis by storing essential commodities with a view to making huge profit. Whatever may be the cause, price hike causes a lot of hardships and sufferings to people. The lower income groups are severely affected by it because they have to maintain their families with their low and fixed income. It is very difficult for them to meet the excessive prices. Usually, they have to buy less or inferior quality items. They find it difficult to make their both ends meet. They have to go without many things that are necessary to lead a normal life. The working class are the worst sufferers. Price hike leads to the widespread corruption and moral degradation. If price hike continues at this prevailing degree, the people of our country may face starvation at a large scale. Price hike is a problem which cannot be solved overnight. All syndicates of the businessmen have to be suppressed boldly and inflation of money should be controlled. Annual budget in agricultural sector has to be increased to increase agri-products. Public awareness should be raised in this respect.

cq_num: A Street Hawker
text:
A person who sells various things moving from street to street, is called a street hawker. A street hawker is a familiar figure in towns. He usually lives in a hut or in a slum. He is the vendor of a variety of things. He carries his things sometimes on the head, sometimes in his hands and at some other times in a small hand-cart. He sells toys, bangles, ribbons, clothings, sweets, fruits, utensils, newspapers, fancy goods and things of domestic use by bringing them up to people's doors. He is a clever fellow who knows how to run his business. He brings one
kind of goods for children, another kind for womenfolk and yet another kind for men. He chooses things for children that will attract children and for women that will attract women. So, he has to be very careful about what things to sell. Thus he earns his living by selling different things to his customers. As he passes along the street, he makes a peculiar cry to attract the attention of his customers. The children here and there gather around him and stop him. The hawker generally chooses a time when the master of the family is away and the women are free from their household duties. He hopes thereby to make a good bargain. In towns everywhere we see hawkers and many of them are making good business. There is another kind of hawkers who buy newspapers, blank pots, old books, worn-out clothes from the houses. However, children are fond of a hawker but men do not like them. By carrying the goods for sale in his hands or on his shoulders, he moves from door to door. Sometimes, he goes from one street to another with his moving shop. A street hawker's life is not easy and comfortable but it is rather difficult and full of hardship.

cq_num: Water Pollution
text: Water pollution means contamination of surface and drinking water to the extent that they are harmful to use. Pure water is essential for health. We cannot live a single day without water. It is the main element of our body. So, the another name of water is life. People pollute water in a variety of ways. Chemical fertilizers and insecticides are used in the fields by the farmers. Rain and floods wash away some of these chemicals and mix them with the water of rivers, canals and ponds. Besides these, mills and factories throw their waste into rivers and canals and pollute water. Oil, food and human waste from boats, launches and steamers get mixed with the water of rivers and canals. A lot of latrines are built on the banks of rivers and canals. A number of kutcha drains run into the rivers and canals. These drains carry human waste and filth and let them fall into water. Thus, it is clear that ignorant people are responsible for water pollution. Polluted water is in no way safe. Water filled with filth, dust and germs is in every way harmful to human life. People get attacked with various waterborne diseases after drinking polluted water. To live a healthy life, we should take all-out measures to minimize water pollution. The first and foremost measure to minimize water pollution is proper management of all sorts of waste. Farmers have to be made aware of using chemical fertilizers and insecticides. Above all, general people should be made aware of the negative effects of polluted water so that they refrain from polluting water.

cq_num: A Village Fair You Experienced
text: A village fair is a common affair in our country. It is generally held every year at a fixed time. It is an annual event. Many people gather in a village fair to buy and sell things. Moreover, it is a great source of recreation to them. A village fair is usually held in an open space to which communication is easy. People from many places come to a village fair to buy as well as to sell their goods. A village fair is a gay affair. I have been to such a fair recently. I was accompanied by two of my bosom friends. There were different types of stalls in different rows, such as: book-stalls, stalls for dolls and toys, electronic goods, hand-made goods, etc. All stalls were almost crowded. We walked round the fair and enjoyed it very much. My friends and I rode on merry-go-round. I bought some toys for my younger sister. A village fair is a place of fun and enjoyment for the children living around. It dispels the monotony of the village people. All categories of people- poor and rich, children and adults, men and women, illiterate and educated çame crowding in the village fair and enjoyed the best of it according to their taste and temperament. The most delighted were the children. They bought cheap flutes, toys, sweets and fancy goods, rode the merry-go-round and feel terribly happy and excited. cq_num: Mobile Phone
text: A mobile phone is one of the greatest inventions of modern science and it has become an essential part of our daily life. It has made communication very easy and quick. In the past, people had to depend on letters or landline telephones to contact others, but now we can talk to anyone at any time from anywhere in the world within a few seconds. A mobile phone is small, light, and easy to carry, so we can keep it in our pocket and use it whenever necessary. It is not only used for making calls but also for sending text messages, taking photos, recording videos, browsing the internet, and using different social media platforms. Students can attend online classes, search for information, and prepare their lessons with the help of a mobile phone. Business people can communicate with clients, send emails, and join online meetings easily. In emergency situations, a mobile phone is very helpful because we can quickly call the police, a doctor, or family members. It also has GPS facilities which help us find unknown places. However, excessive use of mobile phones can be harmful. It may waste valuable time, reduce attention in studies, and cause health problems such as eye strain and headache. Therefore, we should use mobile phones properly and avoid becoming addicted to them. In conclusion, a mobile phone is a wonderful gift of science that makes our life easier and more comfortable when used wisely.

cq_num: A Day Labourer
text: A day labourer is a person who does heavy and unskilled manual work. He is usually employed in agricultural or construction work. So, he needs more strength than skill. He works hard from morning to evening, but he is ill-paid at the end of the day. He cannot meet his bare necessities of life with his scanty income. A day labourer usually lives in a slum and leads a very miserable life with his family members. Though he works hard and works hard and engages himself for the whole day long, he he cannot earn more than his needs. Whenever he car can earn a little bit more, his joy knows no bounds. He buys delicious food and enjoys a good meal with his family. During harvest, he is in great demand and he earns more. But when there is less or no work, his sorrows know no bounds. Sometimes, he has to starve with his family.. Actually, in respect of work, a day labourer has no choice. Sometimes, he is seen coming out of his house with a a spade and a basket. Sometimes, he works in the field, in construction in construction projects, digs soil, breaks bricks, etc. At At the end of his work, he gets his payment and becomes very happy. He tries to save something from this. But when he this. But when he gets no work, his life is at stake. So, the life of a day labourer is not an unmixed happiness. We can teach them about health, nutrition and sanitation. Alongside the government should make special arrangements for them them so that they can buy their daily necessary commodities at cheaper rates.

cq_num: A moonlit night
text: A moonlit night, the full moon shines in its entire glory in the sky, is really charming and enjoyable. It is really a night of dream and it presents a beautiful sight. In the autumn the sky remains cloudless and the moon looks like a big silver disc in the sky. The shiny light of the moon floods the earth and the sky. The moon's rays reflecting on seas, rivers, ponds and hills create a music spell. When the moon pours her light on the waves of water, it sparkles like a diamond. The smooth rays of the moon peace our eyes and mind. In a moonlit night, the grand sights of the canals, rivers, tanks present cannot be described in word. The whole nature looks bright and appears in celestial light. The natural beauty of a moonlit night can better be realized than described. People of all ages enjoy a moonlit night. Young boys play and little kids make funs and amuse themselves. Some people also visit their nearer houses in this night. It seems that the whole nature has taken a new form and everywhere is bright, bright and bright. A moonlit night spreads a feeling of joy in all objects of nature. So, it is really enjoyable for the newly married couple. Poets of all languages have sung highly of a moonlit night. Even, we forget our enmity with others. We make a flock and conversation in different subjects with one another. The moonlit night reminds us the mystery of creation of Allah. We can estimate the moonlit night as offering a charming sight to all living beings. Indeed, a moonlit night is pleasant and fine.

cq_num: A Book shop
text: A book shop is a place where various types of books are sold. In a book shop, generally different kinds of reference books, textbooks, storybooks, novels, books on poems and prose are available. A book shop is important not only for the students and teachers but also for the people of different age groups. Book lovers of all ages gather here and look for what is new in the shop. Usually, every school or college has a book store close to the institution. Besides, some residential areas, where there are many schools, colleges, and universities, also have some book shops. A book shop is arranged in different sections according to different types of books. They are categorically arranged so that the bookseller can find the books easily. Along with books, stationery items such as pens, pencils, erasers, sharpeners, rulers, color paper, binding paper, graph paper, and even dictionaries are found here. The bookseller serves the customer whenever they need him/her. The thirst of readers can be fulfilled by a book shop. A book shop also provides us with facilities to expand our knowledge. Thus, it plays an important role in our life.

cq_num: Contribution of women in a society
text: TODAY'S women are playing important roles everywhere in the world. They are no longer confined within the four walls. They have come out of the kitchen and joined men in all spheres of life. They are becoming pilots, doctors, engineers, teachers, soldiers and many more on receiving higher education. If we look at our country, women are already occupying important positions. Our Prime Minister, parliament Speaker, leader of opposition in parliament and leader of the main opposition political party were all women. Besides, many important positions in the government and multinational corporations are being occupied by women. Women have also have stamped their superiority in games and sports. Recently, we saw brilliant performance of our under-16 women football team. In this way, they are performing creditably in all spheres of our national life and contributing to the economy of the country. Now, everybody has come to realize that true development of the country is never possible by keeping half of the population at home. We only hope that women of the country will continue to prove their worth and make priceless contribution to the development of society.

cq_num: A School Magazine
text: A school magazine is a literary magazine to foster the latent talent of the students, educators and staff of a school. It is also called the masterpiece of a school. Generally a school magazine is published once a year. It usually begins with the foreword of the Headmaster/ the Principal and followed by the Annual Report. The report includes the outstanding result and the excellent performance of a school. It reveals the creative genius and inner faculties of the stakeholders of a school. It is a blend of stories, essays, plays, poetry, puzzles, quizzes, riddles, jokes, comics or any other kind of literary text. The publication of a school magazine committee is not so an easy task. So, in order to conduct the work of the publication of a school magazine, the form of a school magazine committee is a must. The Headmaster/ The Principal is the chief patron of the committee. The committee generally consists nine members. Three of them are teachers, and six of them are students. The Headmaster/ The principal selects the editor from the teachers. And the editor invites the writings from the students, teachers and staffs. After scrutinizing the writings, the best type of writings are selected for printing. But, before going to print, the proof readers have to check the selected writings to avoid mistakes or make them flawless. In fact, a school magazine helps both the students and teachers to develop their power of thinking and writing skills. Hence, it is considered as a key stone to become great writers and poets in the future. In a word, a school magazine reflects the academic, cultural and co-curricular activities as a literary mirror of a school.

cq_num: Air Pollution
text: Air pollution is a major environmental issue that poses a serious threat to life on Earth. It refers to the presence of harmful and unwanted substances in the atmosphere that contaminate the air we breathe. These pollutants mainly include smoke, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, dust particles, and toxic chemicals. The primary sources of air pollution are industrial factories, motor vehicles, brick kilns, power plants, and the burning of fossil fuels like coal and oil. Rapid urbanization and deforestation also contribute significantly to this problem. Air pollution has both immediate and long-term effects on human health. It can cause respiratory diseases such as asthma, bronchitis, lung infections, and even heart problems. Children and the elderly are especially vulnerable. In addition, polluted air harms animals, damages crops, and destroys ecosystems. It also contributes to global warming, the greenhouse effect, acid rain, and ozone layer depletion, all of which have disastrous impacts on the planet. To combat air pollution, it is essential to adopt cleaner technologies, use public transport, enforce environmental regulations, and raise public awareness. Tree plantation and the use of renewable energy sources like solar and wind power are also effective ways to improve air quality. If we fail to address this growing problem, it may lead to irreversible damage to our environment and threaten the future of all living beings.

cq_num: Importance of Learning English
text: English is the most widely spoken and accepted international language in the modern world. It acts as a bridge of communication among people of different nations and cultures. In the field of education, English plays a vital role because most of the world’s books, research works, and scientific discoveries are published in this language. Students who have a good command of English can easily access higher education at home and abroad. In the professional world, English is the language of business, diplomacy, technology, and international organizations. Multinational companies prefer employees who are skilled in English, as it enables smooth communication with clients and partners worldwide. Moreover, English is the main language of the internet, which is the largest source of information, learning, and entertainment. Without English, one cannot fully enjoy the benefits of globalization. Therefore, learning English is not only important for academic success but also essential for building a bright future and becoming an active member of the global community.

cq_num: A Bus Station
text: A bus station is a place where buses arrive, depart, and halt to carry passengers from one place to another. It is usually located in a busy part of a city or town to make transportation convenient for people. A typical bus station has ticket counters, waiting rooms, sheds for shade, and sometimes small shops, tea stalls, and food corners for the convenience of passengers. From early morning till late at night, the station remains crowded with travelers, conductors, drivers, and hawkers. Buses of various routes stand in rows, and conductors call out the names of their destinations in loud voices. Passengers can be seen buying tickets, waiting with luggage, or rushing to catch their buses. The continuous arrival and departure of buses, the honking of horns, and the busy movement of people create a lively but often noisy atmosphere. Porters help carry heavy bags, while hawkers sell snacks, water, and newspapers. Although sometimes overcrowded and chaotic, a bus station is a vital part of our communication system, helping people travel easily, affordably, and quickly from one place to another.

cq_num: A Train Station
text: A train station is an important place where trains stop to pick up and drop off passengers. It is usually full of life, movement, and activity from early morning until late at night. As one enters a train station, the first thing noticed is the ticket counter where passengers buy tickets for their journey. The station building often has a spacious waiting room, restrooms, and sometimes small shops selling food, drinks, and newspapers. On the platform, passengers wait eagerly for their trains, while loudspeakers announce the arrival and departure times. Vendors move through the crowd selling tea, coffee, biscuits, and snacks, creating a lively atmosphere. Coolies dressed in red shirts carry heavy luggage for passengers. Station staff work hard to ensure that everything runs on time and safely. The sight of a train approaching, with its loud whistle and puffing engine, fills the place with excitement. A train station is not only a place of travel but also a meeting point of people from different walks of life, making it an important hub for communication and connection between cities and villages.

cq_num: Environment Pollution
text: Environment pollution is one of the most alarming problems in the modern world. It refers to the contamination of our surroundings by harmful substances that disrupt the balance of nature and threaten the survival of living beings. The major forms of pollution include air pollution, water pollution, soil pollution, noise pollution, and plastic pollution. Air pollution is mainly caused by the smoke emitted from vehicles, factories, and burning of fossil fuels, which increases greenhouse gases and leads to global warming. Water pollution occurs when industrial waste, chemicals, plastics, and untreated sewage are discharged into rivers, seas, and ponds, making water unsafe for humans, animals, and aquatic plants. Soil pollution results from excessive use of chemical fertilizers, pesticides, and dumping of non-biodegradable materials, which reduce soil fertility and damage crops. Noise pollution, created by vehicles, loudspeakers, and industrial activities, harms human health by causing stress, hearing problems, and lack of concentration. Plastic pollution has become a global crisis because plastic is non-degradable and accumulates in land and water, endangering marine life and human health. The consequences of pollution are extremely dangerous, including climate change, rising temperatures, melting of polar ice, destruction of biodiversity, spread of diseases, and scarcity of pure food and water. To combat this issue, governments, organizations, and individuals must work together. Strict laws should be enforced against industries that pollute, and people should be encouraged to plant more trees, use eco-friendly products, recycle waste, and reduce the use of plastics. Public awareness is essential to inspire everyone to protect the environment. If we fail to act in time, our planet will face irreversible damage, making life unbearable for future generations. Therefore, it is our duty to keep the environment clean and safe for all living beings.

cq_num: A bus stand
text: A bus stand is a place where buses stop to pick up and drop off passengers, Usually it is located in busy areas such as marketplaces, near schools, offices, or important roads. A bus stand remains crowded from early morning until late at night. People of all ages and professions gather there to travel to different destinations, while hawkers and street vendors take the opportunity to sell tea, snacks, fruits, water bottles, newspapers, and other small items. The place is always full of activity, with the sounds of bus engines, horns, the shouts of conductors calling out routes, Most of the bus stands have benches or sheds to give shelter from the sun and rain, and larger ones may have ticket counters. Overcrowding is common during office hours, weekends, and festivals, which sometimes causes delays and confusion. Cleanliness is another challenge, as many bus stands become dirty due to litter and waste, creating an unpleasant environment. Pickpockets may also take advantage of the crowd, making it necessary for passengers to remain alert. Despite these problems, a bus stand is an essential part of urban and rural life because it helps people travel quickly and affordably from one place to another. It is not just a point of departure or arrival, but a place where people from different walks of life come together, interact briefly, and then continue on their separate journeys.

cq_num: A Book Fair You Visited / Visiting A Book Fair
text: Every year in February, I visit the Ekushe Book Fair, which is officially known as Amar Ekushey Book Fair. Like the previous years, I have visited the Ekushey Boi Mela. The fair is organized by the Bangla Academy to commemorate the historic Language Movement. The fair is held in the premises of Bangla Academy in Dhaka and attracts thousands of book lovers from all over the country. When I entered the fair, I saw beautifully decorated stalls filled with new books of different genres such as novels, poems, essays, research works, and children’s literature. Famous writers come to the fair to meet readers and sign autographs, which creates great excitement among visitors. The environment of the fair is very lively and inspiring. People of all ages, especially students and young readers, gather there with great enthusiasm. I bought several books written by my favourite authors and also enjoyed cultural programs arranged in the evening. The fair reminds us of the sacrifices made by the martyrs of the Language Movement in 1952. It not only promotes our literature and culture but also encourages people to develop the habit of reading books. Visiting the Ekushe Book Fair was a memorable and enriching experience for me.

cq_num: Prayer for a seat in the school hostel
text:
Date: 10 March 2026
To
The Principal
Bangladesh International School
Nirjhor Cantonment, Dhaka
Subject: Prayer for a seat in the school hostel.
Sir,
With due respect and humble submission, I would like to inform you that I am a student of your esteemed institution. I study in Class X and my roll number is 15. I live in a village which is quite far from my school. Every day I have to travel a long distance to attend my classes. As a result, I often face many difficulties on my way to school. Sometimes I arrive late and cannot concentrate properly on my studies because of the tiring journey.
Moreover, it is very difficult for me to attend extra classes, group study, and other academic activities after school hours due to the long distance from my residence. If I get a seat in the school hostel, I will be able to live in a peaceful and disciplined environment which will help me concentrate more on my studies. I will also be able to follow the rules and regulations of the hostel strictly and maintain good conduct.
My parents are also very eager for me to stay in the hostel so that I can continue my studies in a better way under proper supervision.
Therefore, I earnestly request you to kindly grant me a seat in the school hostel. I shall remain ever grateful to you for your kindness.
Yours obediently,
Anika
Class: X
Roll: 15

cq_num: Subject: Prayer for a full-free studentship.
text:
Dear sir
With due respect, I would like to draw your kind attention to an important matter of mine which I want to resolve with your kind assistance to continue my study.
My father is a poor farmer from a remote area. Carrying the expenses of a six-member family including our four siblings is very difficult for him. Besides, along with me, my three siblings are studying in different public universities of the country. Along with other expenses of the family, the educational expenses of my siblings are about forty thousand taka. But my father's income is only thirty thousand taka only. In such a situation, if my educational expenses are added to those expenses, it will be impossible for my father to spend that amount of money.
Therefore, I hope, in view of the above circumstances, You would kindly grant me a scholarship of tk.3000 per month from the poor fund of the institution and thus enable me to continue my study.
Sincerely yours
Amina
Class viii,
section D

text:
Dear sir,
with humble submission and veneration, we, the Students of your college would like to draw your gracious attention to the the fact that our college common room is not well-furnished and has not any modern facility.
Our common room does not provide much scope of enjoyment and entertainment. Here we do not not get enough facility to enjoy free time as we have not any indoor sports materials such as carom, chess, table tennis etc. Moreover, there are no arrangements of keeping more dailies, magazines, periodical which are part and parcel of academic life. We are also deprived of television, computer and internet facilities. As a result, many of us have to idle away our time. If all the modern amenities are available in our common room, we can keep us engaged and will be able to enrich our extra curricular skill.
Under the above circumstances, we pray and hope that your honour would be kind enough to increase common room facilities and thus give us a chance to keep pace with the modern world.
yours sincerely,
Moni
On behalf of the students of BBN school and college

cq_num: Subject: prayer for permission to go on a study tour.
text:
Dear sir,
With due respect, we, the students of your school would like to draw your gracious attention to the fact that we are willing to go on a study tour on 2nd March 2023 to Sonargaon, the ancient capital city of Bengal.
We feel that an excursion is part and parcel of study. Mere bookish knowledge does not satisfy our thirst for knowledge, if we do not see things with our own eyes. Such a visit to Sonargaon and Panam Nagar will give us a clear idea about our glorious past and its historical evidences. Many people still visit the historic city of Sonargaon and Panam Nagar which have immense beauty and archaeological relics. The Folklore Museum of Sonargaon houses a variety of artifacts from all over Bangladesh, representing the culture and tradition of indigenous group of people. So this tour will undoubtedly widen our knowledge of history and tradition.
It is noted that our teachers have agreed to guide us during the tour and the total costs will be borne by the respective participants. So we need only your permission and hoping you to come with us.
Therefore, we pray and hope that your generosity would kindly give us permission and take the necessary arrangements to make the tour a grand success and oblige us hereby in the days to come.
sincerely yours,
On behalf of the students of BBNSC.
Asif

Banani, Dhaka-1213

Dear Sir,
With humble submission and veneration, I like to draw your gracious and generous attention to the fact that I was a regular student of your school in class VIII in the section of G/B in the session of 2013 . I need a testimonial from you to get admission in a new school/college.
I have passed JSC with a golden A + and have been awarded talent pole scholarship by Barisal Education Board, Bangladesh. My Board Registration No. was 09878765 and my Board Roll number was 11654322 in the exam year of 2013. From my childhood, I t’s my long cherished dream to get myself admitted into a country -reputed institution like Rajuk College or a cadet college. Now, my JSC result once again stirred me to take my dream into reality. Besides, my father works in the Roads and Highways Department under the Ministry of Communications. Recently, he has been transferred to Dhaka with a departmental promotion. As such, our family has already shifted there. May we therefore, pray and hope that your generosity would kindly issue me a testimonial as early as possible and oblige
me hereby in the days to come.
Most obediently yours,
(signature)

cq_num: Prayer for setting up a canteen in our campus.
text:
Date: July 23, 2015
The principal/ Headmaster
Banani Bidyaniketan school & College
Banani, Dhaka-1206
Sub: Prayer for setting up a canteen in our campus.
Dear sir/Madam,
With humble submission and veneration, we, the students of your school like to draw your gracious attention to the fact. It’s a matter of great regret that ours is a reputed institution across the country but we don’t have a canteen in our campus. Our classes begin at 7 am and break up at 3 pm. It is not possible for us to attend classes without a good tiffin. As a result, the students have been facing a great difficulty with the want of a canteen. For the lack of a canteen, the students usually have to take food from the shops of footpath or from outside or from the hawkers. But these foods are not only costly but unhygienic and dirty. On the consequence, most often they have to suffer from mal-digestion, stomachache, diarrhea, dysentery and so many diseases causing from poisonous and adulterated food. Besides, most often we cannot collect the food from outside due to time constraint. So, the necessity of opening/setting up a canteen beggars description.
May we therefore, pray and hope that your generosity would kindly do the needful to set up/open a canteen in no time for the greater interest of the students and school as well and oblige us hereby in the days to come.
Most obediently yours,
On behalf of the students of
Banani Bidyaniketan School and College.
(signature)
Moni/Monir
Class: VIII/IX

cq_num: Subject: Request for immediate action to control the growth of Aedes mosquitoes in our locality.
text:
Sir,
With due respect, I would like to bring to your kind attention a matter of grave concern that demands urgent attention. Our locality is currently facing a severe outbreak of Aedes mosquitoes, which has significantly increased the risk of dengue and other mosquito-borne diseases.
The unchecked growth of these mosquitoes is largely due to the accumulation of stagnant water in drains, roadside ditches, abandoned containers, and construction sites. The situation has deteriorated to such an extent that daily life has become difficult. Children and elderly people are particularly vulnerable, and several residents are already suffering from dengue-related symptoms. Unless immediate and effective steps are taken, the situation may turn into a severe public health crisis.
I, therefore, earnestly request you to take proper measures such as regular cleaning of drains, spraying of mosquito repellents, awareness campaigns, and strict monitoring of mosquito breeding grounds to control the growth of Aedes mosquitoes in our area.
I shall remain ever grateful for your kind cooperation.
Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Address]
[Date]
[Contact Number]

cq_num: Subject: Application for Urgent Repair of Damaged Road at Notun Bazar 100 Feet
text:
Sir,
With due respect, I would like to draw your kind attention to the deplorable condition of the Notun Bazar 100 Feet Road under your jurisdiction. This road, which is a vital route for thousands of commuters every day, has become severely damaged due to continuous heavy rainfall and lack of maintenance.
Large potholes, broken surfaces, and waterlogging have made the road almost unusable, causing great difficulty for pedestrians, rickshaws, and vehicles. Accidents are frequent, and emergency services like ambulances and fire trucks also face serious delays. Moreover, the poor condition of the road is creating heavy traffic congestion and posing health risks due to dust and stagnant water.
We, the residents and regular users of this road, sincerely request you to take immediate action to repair and restore the Notun Bazar 100 Feet Road. Timely repair work will greatly reduce public suffering and ensure smooth and safe transportation in the area.
We hope you will kindly consider our appeal and take necessary steps as early as possible.
Yours faithfully,
Sultan Ahmed
On behalf of the residents of Notun Bazar
Dhaka
Date: 8 August 2025

With due respect, I would like to draw your kind attention to the deplorable condition of the Notun Bazar 100 Feet Road under your jurisdiction. This road, which is a vital route for thousands of commuters every day, has become severely damaged due to continuous heavy rainfall and lack of maintenance.
Large potholes, broken surfaces, and waterlogging have made the road almost unusable, causing great difficulty for pedestrians, rickshaws, and vehicles. Accidents are frequent, and emergency services like ambulances and fire trucks also face serious delays. Moreover, the poor condition of the road is creating heavy traffic congestion and posing health risks due to dust and stagnant water.
We, the residents and regular users of this road, sincerely request you to take immediate action to repair and restore the Notun Bazar 100 Feet Road. Timely repair work will greatly reduce public suffering and ensure smooth and safe transportation in the area.
We hope you will kindly consider our appeal and take necessary steps as early as possible.
Yours faithfully,
Sultan Ahmed
On behalf of the residents of Notun Bazar
Dhaka
Date: 8 August 2025

cq_num: Subject: Application for setting up a canteen.
text:
Sir/Madam,
With due respect, I would like to draw your kind attention to the fact that although our school is large and well-facilitated, it lacks a canteen. The absence of a school canteen has long been felt. During the tiffin break, students often have to depend on food from outside vendors, which is neither hygienic nor safe. Moreover, going outside the campus wastes valuable time and exposes students to unnecessary risks.
In view of the above, it is imperative to establish a well-equipped and well-maintained canteen within the school premises. Such a canteen would ensure the availability of fresh, nutritious, and hygienic food at reasonable prices. This would not only safeguard students’ health but also contribute to maintaining discipline, as everyone would remain within the school boundary during recess.
I, therefore, earnestly request you to take prompt and necessary steps to set up a canteen at the earliest for the greater benefit of the students
Sincerely yours,
Anika
Class–
Section
Roll —

cq_num: Acute Crisis of Water Supply in Kuril Vishwa Road
text:
The water supply in Kuril Vishwa Road area is extremely insufficient. Most of the time the supply remains irregular, and even when it comes, the pressure is so low that it cannot meet the needs of the residents. During the summer season, the problem becomes unbearable. Families suffer badly, students cannot concentrate on their studies, and the poor people are the worst victims. Often people have to wait for hours with pitchers and buckets in long queues for a small amount of water. Sometimes they are compelled to buy water at high prices which is beyond the capacity of many.
We, therefore, earnestly request the authority of WASA to take immediate steps to ensure an adequate and regular water supply in Kuril Vishwa Road. Only then the suffering of the people will be reduced.

Sincerely yours,
Roja

cq_num: Application for Increasing Library Facilities
text:
21 August 2025
The Principal
Viqarunnisa Noon School and College
Dhaka 1216
Subject: Prayer for increasing library facilities.
Dear Sir,
With due respect, I, on behalf of the students of your school, beg to state that our school library is rich in books but not sufficient to meet the growing demand of the students. The number of books is very limited compared to the large number of students. Most of the books are old and torn. We do not have enough reference books on science, mathematics, English grammar, general knowledge, and current affairs. Moreover, the number of daily newspapers and magazines is also insufficient. The reading room is small and cannot accommodate more than thirty students at a time, while the need is for at least eighty to one hundred students.
As the library is the center of knowledge and plays a vital role in improving the academic standard of the students, it is essential to increase library facilities. Supplying more updated books, reference materials, and daily newspapers will enrich our knowledge. Expanding the reading room and providing more chairs and tables will also help us a lot.
Therefore, I earnestly request you to take the necessary steps to increase our library facilities so that we can make the best use of our valuable time in studies.
Sincerely yours,
Disha
Class– 10
On behalf of the students of
Viqarunnisa School and College

cq_num: Application for Sending Relief Goods to Flood-Affected People
text:
21 August 2025
The Mayor
Dhaka North City Corporation
Dhaka.
Subject: Prayer for sending relief goods for the flood-affected people.
Dear Sir,
With due respect, I, on behalf of the people of Amtoli village under Dhaka district , would like to draw your kind attention to the miserable condition of the flood-affected people. Recently, a devastating flood has swept over this region. Houses, roads, schools, and croplands are under water. Many families have become homeless and are passing their days under the open sky without food, pure drinking water, or medical treatment. Children and elderly people are suffering the most. Diseases like diarrhea and fever are spreading alarmingly.
The affected people are in great distress. They are badly in need of dry food, pure drinking water, clothes, medicines, and other emergency supplies. Unless immediate steps are taken, their sufferings will increase beyond measure.
In these circumstances, we earnestly request you to kindly send sufficient relief goods, medical teams, and necessary support to our flood-affected area at the earliest possible time. Your timely action will save many helpless people from starvation, disease, and untold sufferings.
We hope you will take immediate and effective steps to lessen the sufferings of the distressed people.
Sincerely yours,
Roja
On behalf of the flood-affected people
Amtoli, Dhaka

cq_num: An application for sending relief goods and medical facilities to flood affected people.
text:
25 November 2025
The Deputy Commissioner
Patuakhali.
Subject: Request for Relief Goods and Medical Aid for Flood-Affected People
Dear sir,
I am writing to draw your kind attention to the devastating floods that have recently affected our district, Patuakhali. Due to continuous heavy rainfall and overflowing rivers, many villages have been completely submerged, forcing thousands of families to leave their homes. The flood has destroyed crops, homes, roads, and essential infrastructure, leaving the people in extreme hardship.
The situation is particularly alarming for vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, pregnant women, and the sick. Many families are living in temporary shelters without proper food, drinking water, clothing, or medical care. The lack of proper sanitation and medical facilities is increasing the risk of diseases such as diarrhea, cholera, and other waterborne illnesses. Immediate relief is essential to prevent further suffering and loss of life among the affected people.
In view of this urgent situation, I humbly request your esteemed office to provide relief goods and medical assistance to the flood-affected population of our district. Essential items such as food, drinking water, clothing, blankets, and medicines should be distributed as soon as possible to help the victims survive and recover from this disaster.
I sincerely hope that your office will take prompt action to assist the flood-affected people of Mirzagonj, Patuakhali.
Yours sincerely
Asif
on behalf of the people of Mirzagonj, Patuakhali

cq_num: A dialogue between two friends about city life and village life.
text:
Sabina		: How do you spend your days in Dhaka. Anika?
Anika	: Not so good. I feel bored everywhere in this city? I always remember the days in my village.
Sabina	: I think you can't cope with the city life. You can't adjust yourself with the new environment.
Anika		: Yes, You are right. I think village life is easy and pleasing.
Sabina	: But city life is a fast life. Nothing is easy here. You have to make everything easy for you.
Anika	: You have to inhale polluted air. There is a frequent traffic jam. what a disgusting matter it is!
Sabina	: Yes, There is some problems in city life. But you should realise that city offers you every necessary things. You can't get all the advantages in villages.
Anika	: But we have fresh air, open field to play and the beautiful..... cornfields spreading to the horizon.
Sabina	: Absolutely right! But think the other side of village life. you have no modern medical facilities and standard educational institution in the villages.
Anika	: Yes, the villagers are deprived of modern amenities of life but the villagers live peacefully maintaining relationship with the people from all communities.
Sabina	: Yes, there exists a peaceful atmosphere and co-operation among the villagers. It's a good sign.
Anika	: We hardly see such type of co-existence in city life. The inhabitants of the cities are self-centered and remain busy with them themselves. I can't like it.
Sabina		: You quite right!
Anika		: Let's come to the point and do something for the villagers.
Sabina		: Ok, Let's do that.

cq_num: A dialogue between two friends about environmental pollution.
text:
Sabina		: What are you thinking about, Anika? you look so anxious why?
Anika	: Yes, you have understood my mental anxiety. I am concerned about our environment. It  is polluting fast at an alarming rate.
Sabina   	 : You are absolutely right. Our environment is at stake. We are responsible for environmental degradation.
Anika	    	: Shouldn't we find a solution to this problem?
Sabina	:Yes, we should find out the root causes behind the environmental pollution. What do you think about it?
Anika    	: I think emission of green house gases into the biosphere are the main cause of air  pollution. Water pollution, soil pollution and sound pollution are our great concern.
Sabina	    	: How can we solve the environmental pollution. Give your opinion Sabina.
Anika    	: Firstly, We should stop emission of green house gases. We have to find out alternative source of energy.
Sabina    	: You are absolutely right. We can prevent the air pollution by planting more trees  because tree absorb carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases.
Anika	    	: What should we do to stop water pollution?
Sabina	    	: We shouldn't use fertilizer and insecticides more than its proper portion.
Anika    	: The world is ours and keeping the environment in a right track is our responsibility.
Sabina	   	 : Right! Shouldn't we plant a tree sapling in case of cutting a tree?
Anika    	: Definitely! there is no alternative to planting more trees to save our environment.
Sabina   	 : Ok. Let's do that. "Plant more tree and save the environment". This campaign should be  our mission and vision to stop pollution.
