cq_num: Dialogue: Importance of Reading Newspapers
text:
Kamal		: Hello Jamal! What are you doing so early in the morning?
Jamal		: Hi Kamal! I am reading today’s newspaper. It has become my daily habit.
Kamal		: That’s good. But tell me, why do you think reading newspapers is so
important?
Jmal		: Well, newspapers are called the mirror of the world. They provide us with
news and views about what is happening around us and across the globe.
Without newspapers, we would remain ignorant of current affairs.
Kamal		: That’s very true. A student who reads newspapers regularly gains knowledge
of history, politics, economics, culture, science, and technology. It helps to
become smart and well-informed.
Jamal		: Exactly. Newspapers also play a vital role in education. For instance,
students can find reports and features that enrich their essays, debates, and
general knowledge. It also helps improve vocabulary and language skills.
Kamal		: Yes, I agree. I remember last week I used information from a newspaper
article for my school assignment. It impressed my teacher a lot.
Jamal		: Another great benefit is that newspapers make us conscious citizens. They
create awareness about social problems like corruption, drug abuse,
deforestation, and illiteracy. By reading them, we learn how to take
responsibility.
Kamal		: Absolutely. They also keep us updated about government decisions,
economic conditions, and international relations. In this way, newspapers
connect us with the entire world.
Jamal		: That’s right. And you know, newspapers also provide recreation. They have
sports pages, comics, puzzles, and features on culture and literature. So, they
are a source of both knowledge and entertainment.
Kamal		: Very true! For job seekers, newspapers are also useful because they publish
job circulars, exam notices, and important advertisements. Many young
people depend on newspapers to build their career.
Jamal		: Yes. Moreover, newspapers shape public opinion. They highlight the
problems of the people and put pressure on authorities to take action. In this
way, newspapers are considered the “fourth estate” of democracy.
Kamal		: You are absolutely right. So, newspapers are not only a source of
information but also a powerful instrument for social change.
Jamal		: Well said, Kamal. That is why every educated person should cultivate the
habit of reading newspapers daily.
Kamal		: I agree with you completely. From today, I will make it a routine to read the
newspaper every morning.
Jamal		: That’s great! Let’s encourage our friends too. Reading newspapers will
surely help us to become enlightened citizens.
Kamal		: Yes, Jamal. A nation can prosper only if its people are well-informed, and
newspapers play the most vital role in that.

cq_num: A dialogue between me and Anika on visiting a book fair.
text:
Myself		: Hi, Anika! what about you? I went to your house, but couldn't find you.
Anika		: Oh, really? I'm sorry for not finding me at my residence.
Myself		: It's ok. But where did you go?
Anika		: I visited the Ekushe Boi Mela with my father yesterday. I had a very exciting
moment at the Bangla Academy.
Myself		: Absolutely right you are! Visiting a book fair is always interesting and
exciting. More over, it is a Ekushe book Fair.
Anika		: Certainly! Ekushe Bio Mela is the largest book-fair in our country. The fair
is organized by Bangla Academy in commemoration of the language martyrs who laid down their lives for the cause of our mother tongue.
Myself		: Definitely! Ekushe Boi Mela draws the attention of all kinds of readers and
book lovers. It's a month long book fair that is participated by the famous publisters of the country.
Anika		: That's why I couldn't miss the dortunity.
Myself		: How could you enjoy the fair? What's about the visitors of the fair?
Anika		: Oh, I had a wonderful moment. There were as many as two lac visitors
yesterday.
Myself		: How many books did you buy and what were those on?
Anika		: I bought ten books. The books are on the war of liberation, science fiction,
novels and poems.
Myself		: I want to go to the fair tomorrow. Could you please accompany me?
Anika		: Ok, no problem. Which books do you want to buy?
Myself		: I'm always interested in the newly published books. More over, I'm weak at
the famous writers' books like Rabindranath Tagore, Kazi Nazrul Islam, Jasim Uddin and the contemporary writers of our country.
Anika		: Your choice is very fine. I appreciate you that you are going to visit the book
fair. It's your right decision.
Myself	: Thank you that you are agree to go with me. Certainly, we will have a fine day tomorrow at the Bangla Academy.
Anika		: Ok, Let's move.

cq_num: A dialogue between two friends about plans and programs after examination.
text:
Myself		: What is about your plan after JSC examination, Raka?
Sabina	: Thank you for your query about my plan and program. The examination is about to end and I am thinking of my plan. But I have not yet finalized the plans.
Myself	: I have reached a decision about what I should do after the examination.Everything is going smoothly on my part.
Sabina		: what is the plan, Sabina?
Myself	: Oh, you know that every year I go to my village home to spend the vacation with my relatives and friends there.
Sabina		: Is there any special plan at your village home?
Myself	: Of course. I cannot sit idle there. Every moment I remain busy with illiterate people of my village. They are so eager to be literate!
Sabina	: Oh! It's a great plan. We all should go with such kind of plan to educate the people to make the country a literate one.
Myself		: I have also planned for another program.
Sabina		: What is it?
Myself	: I have decided to make a tour to kuakata sea beach. I will go there with my family members. My father will be on leave at that time.
Sabina	: I have also such plan. I always like to pay a visit to an interesting place. Kuakata is a wonderful place.
Myself	: Wonderful! I invite you to go with me. My tour will certainly get a new dimension to have you with me.
Sabina		: Ok! Let's make the plan a success.

cq_num: A dialogue about benefit of early rising/ morning walk.
text:
Sunayna	: Good morning, Sabina! How are you?
Anika		: I'm fine and you?
Sunayna	: I'm fine too. You look so fresh and energetic today. Are you always in a morning walk at this park.
Anika	: Yes, It's my habit. I come here to enjoy the morning air and to take physical exercise very early in the morning everyday.
Sunayna	: Oh, It is a good habit. But I'm not regular like you. I can't rise early
everyday.
Anika	: Why are you late to rise? You are missing the benefit of early rising. It should not be let go.
Sunayna	: Right you are! There are lots of benefits of morning walk. It makes a man healthy, wealthy and wise.
Anika	: Yes, morning walk makes us fresh and cheerful. It also prevents diseases and keeps us happy the whole day.
Sunayna	: Of course, an early riser gets enough time to perform all his duties properly.
Anika		: Is there any benefit of it, Laila?
Sunayna	: Yes, Morning walk refreshes our body and mind. An early riser can enjoy fresh air and get oxygene that are beneficial to health.
Anika		: Shouldn't we grasp the benefits on a regular basis?
Sunayna	: Yes, We should. I want to get the benefit and I will be regular in the morning walk and never be so late in rising early.
Anika	: We should cultivate the habit of early rising. We should also inspire the students to rise early and get the benefits of morning walk.
Sunayna	: Let's do that.
Anika		: Ok!

cq_num: A dialogue between a patient and a doctor.
text:
Patient 	:Good evening, sir! May I come in?
Doctor	: Do Come. Be seated, please, What's your problem?
Patient 	: I feel unwell. I can't eat anything. I don't seem to have any energy.
Doctor	: Ok, I see. Can you be more specific?
Patient 	: I feel tired all the time; Yet I have trouble in sleeping. I have no taste for any food.
Doctor	: You've lost your weight and suffering from malnutrition and sometimes
caught by fever.
Patient 	: Is it very serious, doctor?
Doctor	: Don't worry. It's a simple case. you have to follow just some rules of health
and change your food habit.
Patient 	: Should I have to take high powered medicine?
Doctor	: Not really that. You just take physical exercise regularly and eat healthy
food.
Patient 	: What foods should I eat to get well soon, doctor?
Doctor	: You must have breads, eggs, milk and banana for breakfast. Eat rice, fish or
meat for lunch. You must have more vegetables for lunch and supper.
Patient 	: Should I have anything else?
Doctor	: Oh, Yes, you must drink juice, soup and a lot of water everytime.
Eat more fruits as you can.
Patient 	: Ok, I will follow your prescription.
Doctor	: Don't forget to take physical exercise.
Patient 	: Oh, Yes, I will do. Thank you doctor for your advice.
Doctor	: Welcome. Please report your progress after seven days.
Patient 	: Ok. Bye!

text:
Myself		: Hi! Sumi, What about your examination? Have you wrapped up all your
preparations?
Sumi 		: Thank you friend for your consciousness about my examination. But what
about your preparation?
Myself		: My preparation is yet to complete properly. I have some lackings in few
subjects like English and Bengali. But the preparation of the remaining
subjects are fully completed.
Sumi 		: I am far behind of full preparation I'm doing best to overcome the
shortcomings of each subject. I hope I will be able to minimize the problems
by the and of this month. My subject, teachers are helping me with sincerity.
Myself		: Your attempt to get the full preparations sounds to me with a pleasant mood.
But I think you should make sure of your main weakness. Over all prepation
may bring good sign. But you have to find out your actual weakness so that
you can solve it precisely.
Sumi		: you know how weak I'm in major subjects! So I cannot ignore the normal
subjects lest I should do sorry figure in those subjects. My parents are
anxious about my preparation, but I'm not so afraid of doing dismayed result.
Myself		: Ok, How can I help you if you need? Don't be hesitant.
Sumi		: Thank you for extending your helping hand. We may have a plan to discuss
together on the particular subjects that actually to be discussed. This type of plan
will help both of us.
Myself		: That's a good idea. We may take any help from our subjects' teachers.
Sumi		: Let's do it.
Myself		: Ok! Bye. See you again.

cq_num: A dialogue between me and another person named fahad about aim in life. or choice of a career.
text:
Myself 	: Hello, Fahad! What about you? It's long since we met last.
Fahad		: Oh, fine, I had been in Dhaka since Friday. That's Why I couldn't meet you.
Myself		: Why had you been in Dhaka?
Fahad		: I was there because of my coaching class for being admitted in the University of
Dhaka.
Myself		: Oh, I see you have made up your mind to be a good teacher in a public university.
Fahad		: Yes, it's my ultimate aim to devote my life in educational sector. I have much
pleasure to be so. What's about your plan to be........
Myself		: You know definitely that I'm interested in politics. I want to be a famous and
popular leader of Bangladesh.
Fahad		: How do you materialize your dream? It needs huge leading capacity and dedication
of personal life. What do you think about?
Myself		: You're right. A good politician should be honest. Well disciplined personality with
selfless service, skilled and patriotic. I think I have all these qualities.
Fahad		: Yes, I think so, you're brilliant. But our country's political culture isn't up to the
mark. How could you attain the confidence of the mass people?
Myself		: Your remark coincides with the political culture of Bangladesh. But I want to free
the country from corruption, remove anomalies and irregularities in development
work and satisfy the needs the common people.
Fahad		: That's fine. If all things go on a right way, you certainly will be a great leader of
our country.
Myself		: Thank you Fahad for your wish. May Allah bless us and grant our dream.
Fahad		: Certainly! Allah will be on our side.
Myself		: Let's hope So.

cq_num: On a dailouge on frequent road accidents and it’s causes remidies.
text:
Asif	: Hello! How're you?
Labib	: I'm fine. What about you?
Asif	: Not well. One of my neighbours died last night in a tragic road accident.
Labib	: Very sad! To speak the truth, nowadays it has become a common incident in our country.
Asif	: Yes, it's really a matter of serious concern nowadays but our policy makers seem indifferent to the fact.
Labib	: Absolutely true. But can you tell me the causes of frequent road accidents across the country?
Asif	: The causes are manifold. Reckless driving, lack of skilled traffic police, ignorance of the drivers tendency of overtaking, violation of traffic signals and rules, etc. contribute greatly to the frequent road accidents.
Labib	: You are right. The increasing number of vehicles is also responsible for road accident. With more vehicles, the chances of accidents also rise.
Asif	: Exactly. The traffic in our city is terrible. People don't follow the traffic rules properly. Overspeed is also a major cause of accidents.
Labib	: True. People drive way too fast, even in busy areas or on roads.
Asif	: You said very well, Labib. Overspeeding is one of the leading causes of accidents. When drivers drive in overspeed, accidents take place with sharp turns.
Labib	: You are absolutely right. The conditions of roads are extremely poor. These are also causing many road accidents.
Asif	: Many people drive under the influence of alcohol or drugs, which can easily lead to a fatal crash.
Labib	: That's one of the most serious issues. It’s not just dangerous for the person driving, but for everyone on the road.
Asif	: Have you noticed people using their phones while driving? It is another cause of road accidents.
Labib	: Yes, distracted driving has become one of the leading causes of accidents nowadays.
Right! Immediate steps should be taken to reduce road accidents. What do you think?
Asif	: Yes, of course. Strict laws should be introduced in this regard. Illiterate and careless drivers should be identified, and their driving licenses should be cancelled.
Labib	: Certainly! Besides, drivers should be given proper training, and the Roads and Highways Department should be more cautious in this regard.
Asif	: I agree with you. Also, the government should focus on improving road infrastructure.
Labib	: Definitely. And people should also be encouraged to use public transportation. It reduces traffic congestion and ultimately accidents.
Asif	: Absolutely! People should also be made aware of traffic rules and signals. In this way, we can minimize road accidents.
Labib	: You're right! We all have to come forward to raise public awareness in this concern.
Exactly!
Asif	: Thank you very much for such a nice discussion.
Labib	: You're most welcome. See you again.

cq_num: A Dialogue about having a library card./borrowing a book.
text:
Myself 	: Excuse me! Good morning.
Librarian 	: Good morning ! How can I help you?
Myself 	: I want to have some books  from your library. What procedure I have to
follow?  Please !
Librarian 	: Oh sure!  It's definitely a good idea.You must be a member of our library, Laila. As
a student you should have a Library card to borrow  a book. Now, you take this
form and  fill it up.
Myself 	: OK! Am I required to deposit a membership fee?
Librarian 	: Yes . you have to deposite TK 50 in cash.
Myself 	:   What's  facilities  I will be awarded?
Librarian 	: Oh,you can enjoy so many  facilities from this library. you  can visit the library and
be allowed to stay here for reading books till the  opening time.  you can  also
borrow books, if you wish.
Myself 	: That's great! Library time please?
Librarian 	: The library remains open every day from  10am to 5 pm for the readers.
Myself 	: How many books do you have in your your library and what type of books are in
your collection?
Librarian 	: We have a huge collection of books. These are based on academic,story, novel,
Liberation war and so on! We also have newspapers and magazines.
Myself 	: Wow ! That's fine! I How many books hure can I borrow  from the library and for
how long?
Librarian 	: Yes!  you can borrow two books at a time for two  weeks.
Myself 	: Thank you for the information. I have already filled the form up and deposited the
required fee for the card. Take the form, please.!
Librarian 	: Yes, of course. Thank you so much. Please take your card. I hope that you will be
an active member of our library and maintain all rules and regulations to borrow
books.
Myself 	: Thank you for your help.But I have a question. If I fail to return the
borrowed  books in time, what compensation I will have to pay for the delay?
Librarian	: You have to pay 10 taka for per day delay.
Myself 	: Oh I see! Thanks  a lot for your kind help and information .
Librarian	:  You are welcome. See  you!

cq_num: A Dialogue Between two  friends  About the Importance of Physical Exercise
text:
Rahim		: Hello Karim! How are you doing today?
Karim		: Hi Rahim! I’m doing great. What about you?
Rahim		: I’m fine too. But lately, I’ve been feeling a bit tired and lazy. I think I need
to change something in my daily routine.
Karim		: Oh, really? Have you been doing any physical exercise lately?
Rahim		: Not really. I mostly wake up late and go straight to studying or using my
phone. I hardly move around.
Karim		: That might be the reason. Physical exercise is really important for a healthy
life. It helps you stay active and energetic.
Rahim		: Yes, I’ve heard that before. But I never paid much attention. Can you tell me
more about the benefits?
Karim		: Sure! Regular physical exercise improves our blood circulation, strengthens
muscles, and increases our stamina. It also helps in keeping our heart and
lungs healthy.
Rahim		: That sounds great. But does it help with studies too?
Karim	 	: Of course! Exercise keeps the mind fresh and improves concentration. After
exercising, I feel more focused and motivated to study.
Rahim		: Wow, I really need that! I often feel sleepy and distracted while studying.
Karim		: Then you should definitely start exercising. Even a 30-minute walk in the
morning or some free-hand exercises at home can make a big difference.
Rahim		: But I don’t know where to start. Can you help me?
Karim		: Absolutely! I go for a morning walk in the park near our house. After that, I
do some stretching and basic workouts. You can join me tomorrow if you
like.
Rahim		: That would be great! I think exercising with a friend will also keep me
motivated.
Karim		: Definitely. It’s always more fun when you have a partner. We can also set
some goals and track our progress together.
Rahim		: That’s a good idea. By the way, is physical exercise good for mental health
too?
Karim		: Yes, it is. Exercise reduces stress, anxiety, and even depression. It makes
you feel happy and confident. That’s why doctors and teachers also
encourage it.
Rahim		: I never realized it has so many benefits. Thanks for explaining everything so
clearly.
Karim		: You’re welcome, Rahim. I’m glad you’re taking interest in your health.
Let’s meet tomorrow at 6 a.m. in the park.
Rahim		: Sure! I’ll set my alarm today and get ready. See you tomorrow!
Karim		: See you! And don’t forget your water bottle!

cq_num: A dialogue on the importance of games and sports
text:
Nabin		: Hi Rahul! I saw you playing cricket after school today. Don’t you think you
should be spending more time on your studies, especially with exams
coming up?
Rahul		: Hi Nabin! I do study regularly, but I also believe that playing games is
equally important. A healthy body helps us focus better on studies.
Nabin		: But aren’t games just for fun? I always thought that if we want to do well in
life, we should spend our free time reading or revising.
Rahul		: That’s a common thought, but actually, sports play a big role in our overall
development. When we play, our body stays fit, and our mind feels relaxed.
This makes it easier to study with more concentration.
Nabin		: Hmm, I didn’t think about it that way. But what if someone gets injured or
tired after playing?
Rahul		: Injuries can happen anywhere, even at home. But when we play under
proper rules and with care, the risk is very low. And feeling a little tired after
playing is actually good—it helps you sleep better and wake up fresh the
next day.
Nabin		: That sounds reasonable. But how do sports help us in the long term?
Rahul		: Great question! Games and sports teach us discipline, patience, leadership,
and teamwork. These are life skills we need in every career. Many
successful people say that playing sports helped them build confidence and
manage stress.
Nabin		: I never thought games could teach us so much. I always focused only on
exams and marks.
Rahul		: Marks are important, of course. But physical fitness, mental health, and
strong values are equally important. That’s why schools have playgrounds
and sports periods too.
Nabin		: You’ve really changed my view, Rahul. I think I should take some time out
for sports too.
Rahul		: Absolutely, Nabin! You can start slowly—maybe with evening walks,
cycling, or a simple game with friends. It will help you feel more energetic
and happy.
Nabin		: Thank you, Rahul. From now on, I’ll try to balance studies and sports. Will
you help me get started?
Rahul		: Of course! Come with me to the field tomorrow. You’ll enjoy it for sure.
Nabin		: I’m looking forward to it. Thanks again!
Rahul		: You’re welcome! Let’s stay healthy, study well, and play with joy!

cq_num: Dialogue: Importance of Mobile Phone for Learning
text:
Rafiq		: Good morning, Junaid. How are your studies going?
Junaid	: Good morning, Rafiq. Alhamdulillah, they’re going well. I’ve been
using my mobile phone a lot for studying lately.
Rafiq		: That’s great to hear. You know, I was just thinking about how mobile
phones have become such an essential part of our education nowadays.
Junaid		: Absolutely. In the past, mobile phones were seen mostly as a source of
distraction, but now they are widely recognized as valuable educational tools.
Rafiq		: Yes, especially after the COVID-19 pandemic, we all realized how
important mobile phones are for online learning. Without them, many
students would have been completely disconnected from their education.
Junaid		: That’s very true. Through mobile phones, we can attend live classes, watch
recorded lectures, and access digital resources anytime and anywhere.
Rafiq		: Exactly. I use several apps like Google Classroom, Zoom, and Khan
Academy. They help me understand complex topics more easily.
Junaid		: I use them too. YouTube is also very helpful. I often watch educational
videos there, especially for subjects like science and math. The visual
explanations help a lot.
Rafiq		: Not only that, mobile phones also allow us to read e-books and download
PDFs of our textbooks. It saves time and is very convenient.
Junaid		: Right. And another advantage is communication. We can easily connect
with our teachers and classmates. If I have a question, I just message in our
study group.
Rafiq		: Same here. During exam time, group discussions over messaging apps are
very helpful. We can revise together and share important notes.
Junaid		: But don’t you think there’s also a downside? Many students get distracted
by games and social media when they should be studying.
Rafiq		: Yes, that’s a valid point. Mobile phones are helpful only when used wisely.
If students misuse them, they may waste valuable time.
Junaid		: So, self-discipline is very important. We must use mobile phones as a
learning tool, not as a source of entertainment during study time.
Rafiq		: I completely agree. If we use mobile phones with responsibility and purpose,
they can make learning more effective and enjoyable.
Junaid		: Well said, Rafiq. In today’s digital age, mobile phones are not a luxury but a
necessity for modern education.
Rafiq		: Indeed. They have brought education to our fingertips and made learning
more flexible and accessible.
Junaid		: Let us continue using this technology positively and encourage others to do
the same.
Rafiq		: Definitely. With the right use of mobile phones, we can achieve great
success in our studies.

more flexible and accessible.
Junaid		: Let us continue using this technology positively and encourage others to do
the same.
Rafiq		: Definitely. With the right use of mobile phones, we can achieve great
success in our studies.

cq_num: A dialogue on favourite hobby
text:
Sabina		: Hi Anika! How are you doing today?
Anika		: I’m doing well, Sabina. How about you?
Sabina		: I’m good too. I was just reading a book on hobbies. It made me curious —
do you have a favourite hobby?
Anika		: Yes, I do! My favourite hobby is reading books. I love spending my free
time with books.
Sabina		: That’s really nice. What kind of books do you like to read?
Anika		: I mostly enjoy storybooks, adventure novels, and sometimes biographies.
Reading helps me learn new words and ideas. What’s your hobby?
Sabina		: My favourite hobby is gardening. I’ve been taking care of a small garden at
home for the past two years.
Anika		: Wow! That sounds very peaceful. What do you grow in your garden?
Sabina		: I grow many kinds of flowers like roses, marigolds, and sunflowers. I also
grow vegetables like tomatoes, chillies, and spinach.
Anika		: That’s amazing! Gardening must take a lot of patience and care.
Sabina		: Yes, it does. I water the plants every day, remove weeds, and sometimes add
fertilizer. It feels great to see the plants grow and bloom.
Anika		: I can imagine! Gardening is a very healthy hobby too. You get fresh air and
stay active.
Sabina		: Exactly! It also teaches me responsibility and helps me stay close to nature.
Anika		: Do you do it alone or does your family help you?
Sabina		: Sometimes my younger sister helps me. My parents also encourage me a lot.
They say it’s a good use of my free time.
Anika		: That’s really nice, Sabina. I think I should visit your garden sometime. I
would love to see the flowers and vegetables.
Sabina		: Sure! You can come over this weekend. I’ll show you everything.
Anika		: Thanks! By the way, I think having a hobby is very important for everyone.
Sabina		: I agree. Hobbies help us relax and develop our minds. They also keep us
away from bad habits and laziness.
Anika		: True. Whether it’s reading, gardening, or anything else, a hobby makes our
life more meaningful.
Sabina		: Well said, Anika. Let’s always give time to our hobbies besides our studies.
Anika		: Absolutely! Alright then, see you soon!
Sabina		: See you, Anika!

cq_num: A dialogue about the importance of computer.
text:
Rahim		: Good morning, Karim! How are you doing today?
Karim		: Good morning, Rahim! I’m doing well, thank you. What about you?
Rahim		: I’m fine too. I’ve been thinking a lot about how important computers have
become in our daily lives.
Karim		: That’s a great topic. It’s true that computers are now used in almost every
sector of our society. Can you imagine a day without computers?
Rahim		: Not really. From education to communication, banking to medicine,
everywhere we see the use of computers. It has truly become a part of our
lives.
Karim		: Yes, especially in the field of education. We can attend online classes,
download study materials, submit assignments, and even take exams using
computers.
Rahim		: Absolutely. During the COVID-19 pandemic, students all over the world
were able to continue their education only because of computers and the
internet.
Karim		: That’s right. Computers also help teachers prepare lectures, make
presentations, and evaluate students more easily.
Rahim		: Computers are also widely used in offices. All kinds of record-keeping,
accounting, data analysis, and communication are done with the help of
computers.
Karim		: Yes, and in the business world, computers are used for maintaining
inventory, managing customer information, marketing, and online
transactions.
Rahim		: I’ve read that computers are also used in the medical field—for diagnosing
diseases, maintaining patient records, and even performing surgeries with
high precision.
Karim		: Exactly. Not to mention their role in science and research. Scientists use
computers to perform complex experiments, simulations, and calculations.
Rahim		: And let's not forget entertainment. We can watch movies, listen to music,
play games, and connect with people worldwide using computers.
Karim		: That’s true. Social media, emails, and video calls have made communication
faster and easier, all thanks to computers.
Rahim		: You know, computer programming and coding are also in high demand now.
Many students are learning these skills from a young age.
Karim		: Yes. Having computer knowledge opens up many career opportunities.
That’s why it’s important for us to learn and use computers effectively.
Rahim		: I completely agree. In this digital age, computer literacy is just as important
as reading and writing.
Karim		: Well said, Rahim. We should use computers wisely and try to improve our
knowledge and skills regularly.
Rahim		: Definitely. Thanks for the nice conversation, Karim. I’ve learned a lot from
our discussion.
Karim		: Same here, Rahim. Let’s continue learning and using computers for our
benefit and for the betterment of society.

cq_num: A dialogue about the importance of internet.
text:
Riya		: Good morning, Arman! How are you?
Arman	: Good morning, Riya! I’m fine, thank you. What about you?
Riya		: I’m doing well. I was just thinking about how dependent we’ve become on
the internet nowadays. What’s your opinion about it?
Arman	: That’s a very relevant topic. In my opinion, the internet has become an
essential part of our lives. From education to communication, it is involved
in almost every aspect of modern life.
Riya		: Yes, I agree. During the pandemic, we continued our education only because
of the internet. Online classes, exams, assignments — everything was done
virtually.
Arman	: Exactly. The internet has made education much more flexible. We can attend
classes from home, watch video lectures, and even take online courses from
foreign universities.
Riya		: True. It also helps me with research. Whenever I have doubts or need more
information, I search online. It saves time and effort.
Arman	: That’s right. Search engines like Google, educational platforms like Khan
Academy or YouTube, and digital libraries have made knowledge more
accessible than ever before.
Riya		: Apart from education, I also use the internet to stay in touch with my cousins
who live abroad. We chat regularly and even do video calls.
Arman	: Communication has indeed become easier and faster. Social media
platforms, emails, and instant messaging apps have brought people closer,
no matter where they are in the world.
Riya		: The internet also supports online banking and shopping. My parents often
pay bills and buy things online, which saves both time and energy.
Arman	: Absolutely. Many businesses now operate fully online. The internet has
opened new opportunities in freelancing, remote work, digital marketing,
and more.
Riya		: However, isn’t there a dark side too? Like cyberbullying, data theft, or
wasting time on social media?
Arman	: Yes, the internet has both advantages and disadvantages. It depends on how
we use it. If we are careful and responsible, we can avoid the negative sides.
Using strong passwords, not sharing personal information, and spending
time wisely are very important.
Riya		: That makes sense. So, the internet is like a double-edged sword — useful but
dangerous if misused.
Arman	: Exactly. We should use it as a tool for learning, growing, and connecting
with others - not as a source of distraction or harm.
Riya		: Thanks, Arman. Talking to you really helped me understand the importance
of using the internet wisely.
Arman	: My pleasure, Riya. It’s always good to discuss such meaningful topic.

cq_num: A dialogue on how to eradicate illiteracy problem of Bangladesh.
text:
Alim		: Assalamu Alaikum, Jasim! How are you today?
Jasim		: Wa Alaikum Salam, Alim! I’m good, Alhamdulillah. But I was thinking
about something serious yesterday—our country’s literacy problem. It’s really
worrying.
Alim		: You are right. Bangladesh has made progress in education, but illiteracy is
still a big challenge. Many people, especially in rural areas, cannot read or
write.
Jasim		: Exactly. I think one of the main reasons is poverty. Many children cannot
afford to go to school. They have to work to support their families.
Alim		: That’s true. Poverty forces parents to prioritize earning over education. To
tackle illiteracy, the government should provide free education and stipends to
poor families. This way, children won’t have to drop out.
Jasim		: Yes, financial support can help. But I think another problem is the lack of
awareness. Some parents don’t understand the importance of education,
especially for girls.
Alim		: That’s a big issue too. Communities need awareness programs to encourage
parents to send all their children to school. Local leaders and teachers can
play a vital role in spreading this message.
Jasim		: Also, our schools need better facilities. Many schools in villages don’t have
proper classrooms, toilets, or libraries. Students don’t feel motivated to study
in such conditions.
Alim		: You are absolutely right. Improving school infrastructure is essential.
Moreover, we need more trained and committed teachers who can teach
creatively and make learning interesting.
Jasim		: Another solution is adult education. Many grown-ups missed their chance to
go to school. If we provide evening or weekend classes, they can learn basic reading and writing.
Alim		: That’s a great idea. Literacy campaigns for adults can have a huge impact.
Educated adults can also encourage their children to study.
Jasim		: And we should use technology wisely. Online classes, mobile learning apps,
and radio programs can reach remote areas.
Alim		: True. Technology can overcome distance and shortage of teachers. But the
government and NGOs must work together to ensure every person has access.
Jasim		: So, in short, we need financial support for poor families, awareness
programs, better schools, trained teachers, adult education, and the use of
technology. If we focus on these, I believe illiteracy can be significantly
reduced.
Alim		: Absolutely, Jasim. It will take time, but with determination and proper
planning, Bangladesh can become an educated nation.
Jasim		: Yes, let’s hope for that day soon!

cq_num: A dialogue one causes and effects of water pollution.
text:
Alim		: Hello, Jasim! Have you noticed how polluted the river near our village has
become recently?
Jasim	: Yes, Alim. It’s really alarming. I think water pollution is becoming a
serious problem everywhere, not just here.
Alim		: Absolutely. There are many causes behind it. One major cause is industrial
waste. Factories often dump harmful chemicals directly into rivers and lakes.
Jasim		: That’s true. Besides industrial waste, domestic waste also plays a big role.
People throw garbage, plastic, and even leftover food into water bodies
without realizing the harm it causes.
Alim		: Another cause is agricultural runoff. Farmers use pesticides and fertilizers,
which eventually get washed into rivers and ponds during rainfall. These
chemicals contaminate the water.
Jasim		: I’ve also read that oil spills and sewage disposal are important causes. Oil
spills from boats and ships pollute large areas of water, and untreated sewage
introduces harmful bacteria and viruses.
Alim		: Exactly. These causes not only damage water quality but also affect aquatic
life. Fish, plants, and other creatures cannot survive in polluted water.
Jasim		: And it affects humans too. Contaminated water can cause diseases like
cholera, diarrhea, and hepatitis. Drinking polluted water is very dangerous,
especially for children.
Alim		: Yes, and water pollution also affects agriculture. If polluted water is used for
irrigation, it can destroy crops and make the soil infertile over time.
Jasim		: Not to mention the impact on the environment. Water bodies lose their
natural beauty, and ecosystems become unbalanced. Many animals die, and
some species even face extinction.
Alim		: That’s why we must take action. Public awareness is essential. People
should be educated about the harmful effects of water pollution and
encouraged to keep water bodies clean.
Jasim		: I agree. Governments should also enforce strict laws against dumping
industrial and domestic waste into rivers and lakes.
Alim		: Moreover, promoting the use of eco-friendly products, proper waste
management, and recycling can significantly reduce pollution.
Jasim		: Absolutely. Small steps by everyone can make a big difference. Clean water
is essential for life, and it is our responsibility to protect it.
Alim		: You’re right, Jasim. If we act now, future generations can enjoy pure and
safe water.
Jasim		: Let’s start by spreading awareness in our community. Together, we can fight
water pollution.

cq_num: Dialogue: The Bad Effects of Deforestation
text:
Kamal		: Hello Jamal! You look a bit thoughtful today. What are you thinking about?
Jamal		: Hi Kamal! Actually, I was reading an article about deforestation and its
harmful impacts. It made me really worried.
Kamal		: Yes, you are right. Deforestation is one of the most alarming problems in our
country and also in the whole world. Do you know why it is so serious?
Jamal		: Of course. Trees are called the lungs of nature. If we cut them recklessly, we
are destroying the balance of our environment. For example, deforestation
causes global warming.
Kamal		: Exactly. Trees absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen. If the number of
trees decreases, the amount of carbon dioxide increases. As a result, the
earth’s temperature rises, and climate change becomes more severe.
Jamal		: That’s true. Another bad effect is soil erosion. When trees are cut down, the
soil becomes loose. Heavy rain easily washes away the topsoil, which is very
important for agriculture.
Kamal		: You are absolutely right. This ultimately reduces the fertility of land and
harms crop production. Thus, our food supply also gets threatened.
Jamal		: Yes, and deforestation also destroys the habitats of wild animals. Many
species of birds and animals lose their shelter and food. Some even become
extinct.
Kamal		: That is very sad. I read that the Royal Bengal Tiger in our Sundarbans is
under threat mainly because of the destruction of forest areas.
Jamal		: Exactly. Not only that, deforestation causes floods and droughts. When there
are no trees to hold rainwater, rivers overflow and cause floods. On the other
hand, in dry seasons, lack of trees leads to drought.
Kamal		: Hmm, and floods and droughts are common in Bangladesh. If we don’t
protect our forests, these natural disasters will increase more and more.
Jamal		: Another serious effect is desertification. Places that once were green and
fertile may turn into barren deserts if deforestation continues.
Kamal		: You are right. And we should not forget that forests provide us with timber,
fruits, herbs, and raw materials for many industries. If deforestation goes on,
our economy will also suffer.
Jamal		: Very true. So, I think awareness is the first step. People should know about
the terrible consequences of deforestation.
Kamal		: Yes, and the government should also take strict steps to stop illegal logging.
At the same time, we must plant more trees to replace the ones that are cut
down.
Jamal		: That’s the only solution. If we want to survive, we must protect our forests
and make our environment green again.
Kamal		: Absolutely. Let us take a vow that we will never cut trees unnecessarily and
will always encourage others to plant more trees.
Jamal		: Agreed! Together we can save nature and make a better future.

Jamal		: Yes, and deforestation also destroys the habitats of wild animals. Many
species of birds and animals lose their shelter and food. Some even become
extinct.
Kamal		: That is very sad. I read that the Royal Bengal Tiger in our Sundarbans is
under threat mainly because of the destruction of forest areas.
Jamal		: Exactly. Not only that, deforestation causes floods and droughts. When there
are no trees to hold rainwater, rivers overflow and cause floods. On the other
hand, in dry seasons, lack of trees leads to drought.
Kamal		: Hmm, and floods and droughts are common in Bangladesh. If we don’t
protect our forests, these natural disasters will increase more and more.
Jamal		: Another serious effect is desertification. Places that once were green and
fertile may turn into barren deserts if deforestation continues.
Kamal		: You are right. And we should not forget that forests provide us with timber,
fruits, herbs, and raw materials for many industries. If deforestation goes on,
our economy will also suffer.
Jamal		: Very true. So, I think awareness is the first step. People should know about
the terrible consequences of deforestation.
Kamal		: Yes, and the government should also take strict steps to stop illegal logging.
At the same time, we must plant more trees to replace the ones that are cut
down.
Jamal		: That’s the only solution. If we want to survive, we must protect our forests
and make our environment green again.
Kamal		: Absolutely. Let us take a vow that we will never cut trees unnecessarily and
will always encourage others to plant more trees.
Jamal		: Agreed! Together we can save nature and make a better future.

cq_num: Dialogue: Merits and Demerits of Using Mobile Phones
text:
Anika		: Hello Hira! You look quite busy with your mobile phone. What’s going on?
Hira		: Hi Anika! I was just checking some messages and reading today’s news.
You know, mobile phones have made life so easy.
Anika		: Yes, I agree. One of the greatest merits is that they connect us instantly. We
can talk to friends and family anywhere in the world within seconds.
Hira		: That’s right! Communication has become cheaper and faster. Even video
calls help us feel closer to our loved ones.
Anika		: Another big advantage is education. Mobile phones allow students to attend
online classes, watch tutorials, and download study materials.
Hira		: Exactly! During the pandemic, mobiles became the main tool for education.
They are like small classrooms in our hands.
Anika		: True, but don’t you think mobiles also have some demerits? Many students
waste time playing games instead of studying.
Hira		: That’s a good point. Overuse of mobile phones can lead to addiction. Many
young people spend hours scrolling social media.
Anika		: Yes, and that addiction harms their studies, health, and even their personal
life. Eye problems, headaches, and lack of sleep are common now.
Hira		: Absolutely! But we cannot ignore the positive sides either. For example,
mobiles are helpful in emergencies. One call can save lives.
Anika		: Very true. GPS is also a great merit. While traveling, mobiles guide us and
prevent us from losing our way.
Hira		: Right! Mobile phones also support business. People can buy, sell, or even
work online from home. That has created new opportunities.
Anika		: But on the other hand, mobiles sometimes spoil real-life communication.
People spend more time with their phones than with their families.
Hira		: That’s sad but true. Social media can also spread false news or harmful
content. If used blindly, it misguides people.
Anika		: Another demerit is cybercrime. Hackers can steal personal data through
mobile phones. Many people suffer financial loss.
Hira		: Yes, that’s a serious issue. Moreover, excessive use of mobiles reduces
physical activities. Many people prefer sitting at home with their phones
instead of playing outside.
Anika		: Exactly. That’s why balance is very important. We must use mobile phones
for learning, communication, and safety, but avoid misuse.
Hira		: Well said, Anika. Mobile phones are like a double-edged sword—if used
properly, they are a blessing; if misused, they become a curse.
Anika		: I completely agree. Let’s promise to use them wisely and encourage others
to do the same.
Hira		: Yes, that’s the best way to enjoy the merits and avoid the demerits.
Anika		: I feel much better talking with you.
Hira		: Thanks a lot.

cq_num: Dialogue: The Bad Effects of Smoking
text:
Anika		: Hello Hira! You look very upset today. What happened?
Hira		: Hi Anika! I just visited my uncle at the hospital. He has been suffering from
lung problems because of smoking. It really made me sad.
Anika		: Oh, that’s unfortunate. Smoking is indeed very harmful. It destroys health
slowly and silently.
Hira		: Yes, doctors say smoking damages the lungs, causes cancer, and weakens
the heart. But still many people continue this bad habit.
Anika		: You’re right. Smoking is not just harmful to the smoker but also to the
people around. Passive smokers, especially children, suffer a lot when they
inhale the smoke.
Hira		: Exactly. I read that tobacco contains nicotine, which is addictive. Once
people start smoking, it becomes very hard for them to stop.
Anika		: That’s one of the worst effects. Addiction not only ruins health but also
wastes money. Many people spend a large part of their income on cigarettes.
Hira		: True. Poor families suffer most because instead of buying food or medicine,
smokers waste money on smoking.
Anika		: Another bad effect is on the environment. Cigarette butts pollute the streets,
and smoke pollutes the air we breathe.
Hira		: Yes, and smoking also reduces a person’s lifespan. Smokers often look
older, weaker, and less energetic than non-smokers.
Anika		: Very true. It also creates social problems. Many schools and offices forbid
smoking, but some people still do it secretly, setting a bad example for others.
Hira		: That’s right. Teenagers often start smoking just to look smart or stylish, but
later they regret it when their health gets damaged.
Anika		: I completely agree. Smoking also causes bad breath, stained teeth, and an
unpleasant smell, which makes people socially uncomfortable.
Hira		: Yes, Anika. My uncle said he wishes he had never started smoking. Now he
is suffering and spending a lot of money on treatment.
Anika		: That is so sad. If people were more aware, they would avoid this deadly
habit. Governments also warn people by printing “Smoking is injurious to
health” on cigarette packets.
Hira		: Unfortunately, many ignore these warnings. They think smoking gives them
relaxation, but in reality, it slowly kills them.
Anika		: Absolutely! Life is precious, and health is wealth. Smoking steals both. It is
better never to start, because prevention is easier than cure.
Hira		: Well said. Let us always say no to smoking and encourage others to quit this
dangerous habit.
Anika		: Agreed, Hira. Together we can spread awareness and save lives.

cq_num: Dialogue on Advantages and Disadvantages of rural life and urban life.
text:
Tabassum	: Hello Faria! How are you today?
Faria		: I’m fine, Tabassum. What about you?
Tabassum	: I’m good too. I was just thinking about the differences between village life
and city life. Do you prefer living in a village or in a city?
Faria		: That’s an interesting question. Honestly, I feel village life has many
advantages. It is calm, quiet, and free from pollution. The green fields, fresh
air, and simple lifestyle make it very peaceful.
Tabassum	: You’re absolutely right. In villages, people can enjoy the beauty of nature.
They get fresh vegetables, fruits, milk, and fish. Life there is simple and less
stressful compared to the city.
Faria		: Yes, people in villages are cooperative, friendly, and helpful. They live close
to each other, share joys and sorrows, and maintain strong social bonds. Don’t
you think that’s a big advantage?
Tabassum	: Of course. But village life also has some disadvantages. There are not
enough schools, colleges, or universities in rural areas. So students often have
to go to towns or cities for higher education.
Faria		: That’s true. Besides, villages lack modern hospitals, doctors, and medical
facilities. In times of emergency, it becomes really difficult for villagers to get
proper treatment.
Tabassum	: Exactly. On the other hand, city life is full of facilities. In cities, you can find
good educational institutions, modern hospitals, and plenty of job
opportunities.
Faria		: Yes, cities also have better communication, electricity, transportation, and
entertainment. People can enjoy theatres, shopping malls, restaurants, and
cultural programs.
Tabassum	: That’s right. But city life has its disadvantages too. The roads are always
crowded with vehicles, and traffic jams waste a lot of valuable time.
Faria		: Not only that, but the air and water in cities are polluted. Factories, smoke,
garbage, and noise make life unhealthy. People often suffer from stress,
tension, and various diseases.
Tabassum	: Another problem is that life in cities is too fast and competitive. People are
always busy with their own work. They hardly know their neighbors and
rarely have time for social bonding.
Faria		: True. That’s why many people call city life mechanical. In contrast, villages
may not have all the facilities, but they have peace, honesty, and unity.
Tabassum	: Well said, Faria. So I think both village and city life have advantages and
disadvantages. Village life is peaceful but lacks facilities, while city life is
comfortable but full of problems.
Faria		: I agree. If we could combine the good sides of both, life would be really
perfect.
Tabassum	: Absolutely. Let’s hope for a balanced lifestyle in the future.
Faria		: Yes, that would be wonderful.

cq_num: The bad effects of adopting unfair means in the exam
text:
Myself	: Hello Asfia! You look worried today. Is everything okay?
Asfia		: Hi! I’m a bit stressed. Our exams are coming soon, and I keep thinking
about all the pressure.
Myself	: I understand. Exams are always stressful. But we should face them honestly.
Asfia		: I know, but sometimes I wonder if using unfair means could help me get
better marks.
Myself	: Oh Asfia! That’s not a good idea at all. Adopting unfair means in exams can
have many bad effects.
Asfia		: Really? I think if I cheat just once, no one will know, and I can score well.
Myself	: That may be true for a moment, but in the long run, it is very harmful. First
of all, it destroys your honesty and integrity. Once you start cheating, it
becomes a habit.
Asfia		: I never thought about it like that.
Myself	: Yes, and cheating prevents you from actually learning. If you use unfair
means, you may get good marks, but you won’t gain real knowledge. That can
affect your future education and career.
Asfia		: That’s true. I guess I won’t understand the lessons properly if I rely on unfair
means.
Myself	: Exactly. Moreover, if you are caught cheating, the consequences can be very
serious. You may fail the exam, be punished by the school, or even be banned
from exams. It can ruin your reputation forever.
Asfia		: Oh, I didn’t realize that. I thought it was just a small thing.
Myself	: Small things can become big problems. Also, cheating can make you feel
guilty and anxious. Constant fear of being caught increases stress and affects
mental health.
Asfia		: I see. So, it’s really not worth it.
Myself	: Absolutely. Instead of adopting unfair means, we should focus on hard
work, regular study, and time management. Success achieved honestly gives
true confidence and satisfaction.
Asfia		: You are right. Hard work is the real key to success. I should start preparing
honestly from today.
Myself	: That’s the spirit! Remember, exams are not only about marks; they are about
learning and improving yourself. Cheating may give temporary success, but
honesty always brings long-term benefits.
Asfia		: Thank you for guiding me. I will never try unfair means again. I’ll work
hard and trust my abilities.
Myself	: Good decision, Asfia. Believe in yourself, and you will do well!

cq_num: Dialogue on Early Rising and disadvantages of Late  Rising.
text:
Tabassum	: Good morning, Faria! You look fresh today. Did you wake up early?
Faria		: Good morning, Tabassum! Yes, I woke up early today. I believe early rising
has many benefits.
Tabassum	: Absolutely! Waking up early makes you feel active and energetic throughout
the day. You can complete your tasks on time and stay healthy.
Faria		: Yes, I also think early risers enjoy the quiet morning hours. It’s peaceful,
and they can study, meditate, or plan their day properly.
Tabassum	: That’s true. Early rising also improves concentration and discipline. People
who wake up early tend to be more productive and successful in life.
Faria		: I agree. Fresh air in the morning is good for health, and exercising in the
morning keeps the body fit. Early risers also avoid the stress of rushing in the
morning.
Tabassum	: On the other hand, some people prefer late rising. They sleep late at night
and wake up later in the morning.
Faria		: Yes, and late rising can have its advantages too. Some people feel more
creative and active during the evening and night. Their mind works better
when they follow their natural body rhythm.
Tabassum	: That’s interesting. Late risers can stay up late to study, work, or complete
creative tasks without disturbance. Some writers, artists, and thinkers prefer
night hours for concentration.
Faria		: Exactly! Late rising allows flexibility. People who wake up late can enjoy
longer rest and sleep properly, which is also essential for health.
Tabassum	: True, but late risers may lose the quiet and fresh morning hours, and their
day may feel shorter. They sometimes miss morning sunlight, which is good
for the body.
Faria		: That’s correct. So, both early rising and late rising have their own benefits. It
depends on a person’s lifestyle, work, and body rhythm.
Tabassum	: I agree. But personally, I prefer early rising because it makes me disciplined,
energetic, and more productive.
Faria		: And I like late rising sometimes because it gives me creativity and proper
rest. Everyone can choose what suits them best.
Tabassum	: Absolutely! Balance is important, but waking up early definitely gives an
edge in health and success.
Faria		: True! Early or late, the key is to manage time wisely and stay healthy.

cq_num: A Dialogue on Pastime
text:
Anika	: Hello, Tabassum! How are you today?
Tabassum	: Hello, Anika! I am fine, thank you. What about you?
Anika	: I am quite well. I was just thinking about how people spend their free
time. Tell me, what do you usually do in your leisure time?
Tabassum	: In my free time, I usually read storybooks and sometimes listen to music.
Reading books is my favourite pastime. What about you?
Anika	: My favourite pastime is gardening. Whenever I get spare time, I work in
our small garden at home. I take care of flowers and plants. It gives me
great pleasure.
Tabassum	: That sounds really interesting. Gardening is not only enjoyable but also
very useful. It makes our surroundings beautiful and refreshing.
Anika	: Yes, exactly. When I work in the garden, I feel very relaxed and happy. It
removes my mental stress and refreshes my mind.
Tabassum	: That is wonderful. Reading books also gives me great joy. When I read \ interesting stories, I feel as if I am visiting different places and meeting
different people.
Anika	: What kind of books do you prefer to read?
Tabassum	: I mostly like adventure stories and biographies of famous people. These books inspire me and teach me many valuable lessons about life.
Anika	: That is a very good choice. Books are truly our best friends. They increase our knowledge and help us understand the world better.
Tabassum	: You are right. Besides reading, sometimes I also watch educational programmes on television. They are quite informative.
Anika	: That’s good. But many students nowadays spend most of their free time on social media and mobile phones.
Tabassum	: Yes, I have noticed that too. Spending too much time on mobile phones is not a good habit. It wastes time and distracts us from study.
Anika	: Exactly. A good pastime should refresh our mind and also be beneficial for us.
Tabassum	: I completely agree with you. Pastime plays an important role in our life. It helps us remove boredom and gives us new energy for our work.
Anika	: Yes, everyone should have a healthy and useful pastime according to their interest.

cq_num: A Dialogue on Physical Exercise
text:
Tabassum	: Hello Hira! You look very energetic today. Where are you coming from?
Hira	: Hello Tabassum! I have just returned from the playground. I was doing some physical exercises with my friends.
Tabassum	: That’s great! I have heard that physical exercise is very important for our health, but I don’t do it regularly.
Hira		: You really should start doing it. Physical exercise keeps our body fit and active. It also refreshes our mind.
Tabassum	: But I often feel lazy in the morning. That is why I cannot wake up early to exercise.
Hira		: At first it may feel difficult, but once you make it a habit, it becomes easy. Even a simple morning walk or some stretching exercises can be very helpful.
Tabassum	: What kinds of exercises do you usually do?
Hira	: I usually start with jogging for a few minutes. Then I do some free-hand exercises like stretching, bending, and breathing exercises. Sometimes I also play badminton or run in the field.
Tabassum	: That sounds interesting. Does it really help you stay healthy?
Hira	: Of course. Physical exercise improves blood circulation and strengthens our muscles and bones. It also helps digestion and keeps away many diseases.
Tabassum	: I see. Nowadays many students spend most of their time using mobile phones and computers. They hardly do any physical activities.
Hira	: You are right. That is why many young people feel tired and weak. Without physical exercise, the body becomes inactive and unhealthy.
Tabassum	: How long should we exercise every day?
Hira: Doctors usually suggest at least thirty minutes of exercise daily. It can be walking, cycling, playing games, or any other physical activity.
Tabassum	: I think I should start exercising from tomorrow morning.
Hira		: That’s a very good decision. If you want, you can join me and my
friends in the playground.
Tabassum	: That would be wonderful. Exercising with friends will surely make it more enjoyable.
Hira	: Exactly. Physical exercise not only improves our health but also makes us cheerful and confident.
Tabassum	: Thank you, Hira. Your advice has really inspired me.
Hira	: You are welcome, Tabassum. Remember, a healthy body is the key to a happy life.

cq_num: A Dialogue on the Importance of Learning English
text:
Rafi	: Hello Nabila! You seem very busy with your English book. What are you studying?
Nabila	: Hi Rafi! Yes, I am practicing English. Our teacher said that learning English is very important for our future.
Rafi	: I know English is useful, but sometimes I feel it is difficult. Why do you think it is so important?
Nabila	: There are many reasons. First of all, English is an international language. People from different countries use English to communicate with each other.
Rafi		: That is true. When people from different nations meet, they usually speak in English.
Nabila		: Exactly. If we learn English well, we can communicate with people all over the world. It helps us understand different cultures and ideas.
Rafi	: I have also heard that most books on science, technology, and higher education are written in English.
Nabila	: Yes, that is another important reason. If we know English, we can read many valuable books and gather knowledge easily.
Rafi	: That means English helps us in education as well.
Nabila	: Certainly. Many universities around the world use English as the medium of instruction. Without English, it becomes difficult to study abroad or even access online courses.
Rafi	: I see. English must also be important for jobs.
Nabila	: Of course. In many professions such as business, medicine, engineering, and information technology, English is widely used. A person who knows English well often gets better job opportunities.
Rafi	: That makes sense. Even in our country many offices prefer employees who can speak English fluently.
Nabila	: Yes. Moreover, English is also the main language of the internet. Most websites, research articles, and international news are in English.
Rafi: 	So if we know English, we can learn many things from the internet and stay updated with the world.
Nabila	: Absolutely. Besides, learning English improves our confidence. When we can speak English properly, we can express our ideas clearly in front of others.
Rafi	: You are right. Now I realize that learning English is not only for passing exams but also for building our future.
Nabila	: Exactly, Rafi. If we practice regularly, English will become easier for us.
Rafi		: Thank you, Nabila. From today I will try to practice English more seriously.
Nabila	: That’s great! Remember, the more we practice, the better we learn.

cq_num: A Dialogue About Opening a Bank Account
text:
Rahim			: Good morning, sir. I would like to open a bank account. Could
you please tell me the procedure?
Bank Officer	: Good morning. Certainly, I will help you. First of all, may I know what type of account you want to open?
Rahim			: I am a student, so I think a savings account will be suitable for me.
Bank Officer	: Yes, a savings account is a good choice for students. It allows you to deposit money safely and earn a small amount of interest.
Rahim			: That sounds good. What documents do I need to open the account?
Bank Officer	: You will need a photocopy of your national ID card or birth certificate, two passport-size photographs, and an introducer who already has an account in our bank.
Rahim:			 I see. I have brought my birth certificate and photographs with me.			 My uncle also has an account here. Can he be my introducer?
Bank Officer	: Of course. If your uncle signs the introduction section of the form, that will be enough.
Rahim		: Thank you. Is there any minimum amount required to open the account?
Bank Officer	: Yes, you have to deposit at least 500 taka as the initial amount for a savings account.
Rahim		: That is quite reasonable. After opening the account, will I get a debit card?
Bank Officer	: Yes, you can apply for a debit card. It will allow you to withdraw money from ATM booths and make payments easily.
Rahim			: That will be very helpful. Will I also receive a cheque book?
Bank Officer	: Yes, we will provide you with a cheque book after the account is activated.
Rahim			: How long will it take to open the account?
Bank Officer	: If you fill up the form properly and submit the required documents, the account can be opened within one or two working days.
Rahim		: That is very convenient. Please give me the form. I will fill it out right now.
Bank Officer	: Here is the form. Fill it up carefully, and if you face any difficulty, feel free to ask me.
Rahim			: Thank you very much for your kind help.
Bank Officer	: You are welcome. We are always happy to assist our customers.

cq_num: 1. A letter to your friend congratulating him on his brilliant success.
text:
25 March 2025
Banani Dhaka
23/A Banani Model Town
Dear Amina,
I feel much pleasure and thrilled that you have brightened everyone's face by scoring outstanding ing results in the last final examination. So I express my, heartiest congratulations to you on your brilliant success.
I am proud to hear that you have secured distinction in all subjects surpassing all the examinees of your school. Your sincerity and Keen attention to studies and direct guidelines and advice of the teachers that paved the way for your grand success. I cannot express in words how happy I am with your wonderful results! I am truly excited, overwhelmed and thrilled today.Today, I would be happy and delighted, if I could be by your side at this auspicious moment.
Once again, I congratulate you from the core of my heart on your extraordinary results. Your result will be a source of inspiration in my academic life. May your success in future be continued.
With lots of love!
Sabina

cq_num: Write an email to your friend thanking him for sending you a nice gift on your birthday.
text:
monira78@gmail.com
Subject: Thanks for sending a nice gift.
Dear Monira,
You made an apology for not being able to attend my birthday party due to your urgent business. But on the other hand, you sent a lovely gift on my birthday. Your gift has been my heart's content. This is one of the best gifts in my life that has touched my heart.
Birthday gifts from someone are very precious and such a gift from you is definitely a special one. SO thank you very much for the gift sent to me. You know, I love to read books, especially juvenile novels. The gift you sent me is a hit teen novel by a famous author.I was thrilled to read this book. I kept the gift you sent me very carefully in my bookshelf. Whenever I have time, I read the book and get much pleasure.
Once again, I earnestly express my sincere thanks and gratitude to you for sending me the gift. I hope to see you very soon. Then there will have a lot of talks and fun in the meeting
Your love.
sabina

cq_num: About your Aim in lfe
text:
50/A
Banani, Model Town
Dhaka —1213
18 April May 2025
Dear Anika
I hope that this letter finds you in great health and your family is well too. I am good here. Yesterday I attended an event that was held at our school. The topic for the same was ‘Aim in Life’. It was such an event that provided great perspective. After that, my aim in life, which is to be a doctor, became a lot more prominent.
Doctors are no less than superheroes in today’s world. Especially after witnessing situations like COVID-19, and seeing how selfless the service of doctors is, my respect for them has increased to a whole new level. Now, my aim in life, which is to be a doctor, has become of utmost importance to me. To me, being a doctor is not just about being in a good profession, it is also about giving back to the community in the form of noble service to people. No matter how hard it is to reach it, I will accomplish my aim in life, I will become a great doctor. And for that, I am ready to study as much as required, dedicating as much time as the profession demands.
I hope that you too are dedicated to the goal that you have set for your life, because dedication is the key to achieving it. It has been a long time since we’ve met. So when you get time, let us catch up and plan an outing together.
I miss you a lot. Hope to see you soon. Till then, take care.
Your loving friend,
Sabina

cq_num: Younger brother telling him how to improve his competency in English.
text:
25/A
Banani Model Town
Dhaka —1213
25 April 2025
Dear Akash,
You know English is used and understood in almost all over the countries. Nowadays, more and more people are dedicating time to studying English as a second language. Many countries include English in their school syllabus and children are starting to learn English at a younger and younger age. But what is the true value of learning English? Whether you are looking for a new job or planning to travel the world, studying English can help you progress in life both personally and professionally. You can increase your career opportunities and start to meet people around the world.
However, do you know why learning English is so important? If you wish to have higher education in any subject you must know English. So try to improve your English. By listening, speaking and reading books, you can improve your English. I advise you to read English newspaper and listen to English news bulletin regularly. Read story books and novels. Try To speak English as much as you can. Your speaking English on regular basis definitely will help you speak fluently. If you follow these instructions, you will surely improve.
That’s all for today. Please convey my respectful compliments to our beloved parents and great love to my younger sister. I miss you so much.
With best wishes.
Anika
Now, write a letter to your friend describing the co-curricular practice / activities of your school.
25/A
Banani Model Town
Dhaka -1213
12 April 2025
My dear Arnob,
Cordial love to you. Hope, you are all OK by the grace of Almighty Allah. I received your long-felt letter yesterday. In your letter you wanted to know about the co-curricular activities/ practices of our school. Now, I am going to write a short description about the same.
Besides academic activities, co-curricular activities of our school are all along getting important. We regularly play football, cricket, table tennis, badminton, volleyball and many other games. We take part in indoor and outdoor games. We work in our school garden by turns. We take part in symposiums, debates, extempore speeches, selected speeches arranged by our school. We regularly publish quarterly, half-yearly and annual magazines. We are also to take part in debating and drawing competitions. Every year we go on a picnic and study tour. Throughout the year, our teachers keep us busy with the co-curricular activities. We feel much interested in doing these activities.
That’s all for today. Please tender my salam to your parents and love to youngsters. Thank you for your letter. More when we meet. Write to me at your earliest convenience. I miss you so much.
Yours ever,
Jakia

cq_num: An email to your friend about the importance of reading newspapers.
text:
To: zhang14@yahoo.com
Subject: The importance of reading newspapers.
Dear Anik
Perhaps you are getting on very well with your studies. Sorry for the delay in acknowledging your emails because of my ongoing exams. In your email, your curiosity about the importance of reading newspapers stirred me to write something about it.
You know that by reading newspapers, we can get the news of various things of the world. . It brings us the whole world to our door steps in terms of education, business, weather, science, medicines, Literature, politics, games and everything. In a word, the importance of reading newspapers beggars description.so, you must have the habit of reading newspapers regularly.
Anyway, we miss you all the time. We are very eager to see you in the midst of us soon. So, have a visit to our home at your earliest convenient time. Let us know early about your arrival so that we can receive you at the airport. By the way, we want to see auntie and Tanu with you. So, don't forget to bring them with you this time.
I must end now. We are waiting impatiently to hear from you very soon.
Wishing you all the best.
Your loving friend
Anika

text:

Dear Monica,

cq_num: Write a letter to your friend describing the importance of physical exercise
text:
25 May 2025
23/A Banani
Dhaka -1213
Dear Anika,
At the outset of my letter, take my cordial love. I hope you are well by the grace of almighty Allah. I am also well. I am very sorry to learn that you are not conscious about your health. Hence, you are suffering from many diseases. I have also learnt that you do not take physical exercise. Today I shall tell you about the importance of physical.
Physical exercise is very necessary to us. Our life is full of activities and actions. One can tackle them by his physical fitness. It improves our health and makes a man strong and active. There is a proverb, 'A sound mind lies in a sound body" If the body is unsound, the mind must be unsound. A sound body depends on regular exercise. Physical exercise is the most important for the students. They do hard mental work. So they need to sound body and sound mind which depend on regular exercise. Those who do not take regular exercise, soon they loss their health. They do not find interest and happiness in life. Health is wealth. It is the root of all happiness. Physical exercise enables us to achieve this wealth. None can enjoy good health without physical exercise. It also keeps us free from all types of diseases. So you should take physical exercise regularly to keep your body and mind fit for work.
That's all for today. More when we will meet. Convey my best regards to the parents and love for the youngers. Write to me at the earliest convenience. I miss you so much.
Your love
Samiha

cq_num: A letter to your friend describing the picnic you have enjoyed.
text:
25 May 2025
23/A
Banani, Model Town
Dhaka -1213
At the outset of my letter, take my cordial love. I hope you are well by the grace of almighty Allah. I am also well. You will be very glad to know that we had a picnic at Sonargaon in Narayanganj. It is a historical place. It was once the capital of Bengal. We went there last Sunday. Now I am giving you a short description about it.
The picnic spot was really nice. We hired a bus to carry us. We started at 7.a.m from our school gate. After having reached there, we finished our breakfast by 9.30 am. We did not take all the necessary food stuff and utensils with us. There was an excellent arrangement for our lunch there. We enjoyed heavy and delicious food items there. At the initial stage of Asif picnic, we visited the Panam Nagar. We stayed there 2 hours. We saw many archaeological relics and ancient abandoned buildings. After visiting the Panam Nagar, we came to Sonargaon folk museum. We went round the place and spent a few hours in sight-seeing. We also played cricket there about 30 minutes. The museum of Sonargaon charmed us most. We started for home at 5 pm after having snacks and tea. It was an enjoyable picnic. It would be more enjoyable if you accompanied us.
That's all for today. More when we will meet. Convey my best regards to your parents and love for the youngers. Write to me at your earliest convenience. I miss you so much.
Your loving friend,
Asif

text:
26 May 2025
20/A Banani
Road No--23
Dhaka-1213
Dear Emon
How's it going? Perhaps you are getting on well with your studies and enjoying a fab time in Khuln. Sorry for delay in acknowledging your wonderful sweet note and your grand hospitality that we received during our visit to your house.
Really, I am very much fascinated in receiving your amicable hospitality from you. Every member of my family and my friends were very much impressed to enjoy such a nice generosity and lavish hospitality during our stay in khulna. Everyone praised it a lot. Truly speaking, the members of your family cared for us and received us. So we had a wonderful time at your residence. I will never forget such type of cordial hospitality given to us by you and your family. So I once again thank you from the core of my heart.
Anyway, I must end now. Please send my regards to the parents and affection to the youngsters. Write to me at your earliest convenience. I am waiting impatiently to hear from you very soon. I miss you so much.
Your loving friend,
Hasif

cq_num: Regarding annual prize giving ceremony.
text:
26 July 2025
23/A Banani
Dhaka--1213
Dear Sabrina,
It is very pleasure to describe the annual prize giving ceremony of of our school held on 20th January last. The function impressed me so deeply that I could not but share some important events with you. So. I am penning down something about it.
On the eve of the very day, the school buildings were beautifully decorated with banners, colourful flowers and pictures. The auditorium and its dias were also brightly decorated. The prizes were neatly displayed on the dias. All the students were present at the function Among others the guardians and some eminent personalities of the locality were present. At beginning of the function, the headmaster read out the annual report of the school. Then the chief guest delivered a short speech and gave away the prizes among the winners. I got two prizes – one for regular attendance and other for outstanding result in the last annual examination. Finally, the day's program ended with an amazing musical soiree. The an ama-musical soiree. We all enjoyed the day very much.
That's all for today. With love and kind regards to your parents and affection to the youngers. Write to me at your earliest convenience.I am impatiently waiting for your reply. I miss you so much.
Monica

cq_num: Write a Letter to your friend about what you will intend to do after SSC examination.
text:
123, College Road
Dhaka
7th June 2025
Dear Asif,
At the outset of my letter, take my cordial love. I hope you are hale and hearty by the grace of Almighty Allah. I received your letter yesterday and became very happy to know that your SSC exams also went well. Like you, I have also completed my SSC examination successfully, and now I am enjoying a well-deserved break after a long period of study and pressure.
You will be glad to know that I have made some fruitful plans for the time after the SSC exam. First and foremost, I want to spend some time relaxing and recharging myself, as we have been under a lot of pressure during the last few months. After taking a short rest, I plan to enroll in a computer course. I believe that in today’s world, having digital skills is extremely important. I am particularly interested in learning graphic design and basic programming, which I think will help me in my future academic and career pursuits.
If everything goes well, I also wish to go on a family tour to Cox’s Bazar or Sylhet. This will refresh my mind and give me an opportunity to enjoy the beauty of nature. Coming back from the special tour, I have made up my mind to get myself admitted into an English Language Club to enrich my English skills.
That's all for today. Let me know about your plans after the SSC examination. I hope we will be able to meet soon and spend some quality time together.
With best wishes and love,
Your loving friend,
Mehedi

cq_num: Write a Letter to Your Father about Your Preparation for the Upcoming SSC Examination
text:
23/A Banani
Dhaka, Bangladesh
20 August 2025
My Dear Father,
I hope this letter finds you in good health and cheerful spirits. I am also fine here by the grace of Almighty Allah. You will be glad to know that my preparation for the upcoming SSC examination is going on in a systematic way. As the days of the examination are drawing near, I am working harder than ever to make a good result.
I have already completed the syllabus of most subjects. In English, I practice writing, grammar, and reading daily. In Bengali, I revise grammar, essays, and important poems and stories. I have finished almost all the chapters of Mathematics and now I solve problems regularly to make myself confident. In Science subjects, I read the important topics carefully, memorize formulas, and draw diagrams. I also revise Social Science and Religion regularly.
Dear Father, I follow a strict routine to divide my time equally among all subjects. I wake up early in the morning and study with full concentration. I try to avoid wasting time and maintain good health as well. My teachers are very satisfied with my progress and encourage me to continue my effort.
Please keep me in your prayers so that I can do well in the SSC examination and make you proud. Convey my respect to Mother and love to younger ones.
Yours loving son,
Anika
