cq_num: Letter to a Friend Inviting Him to Visit Bangladesh
text:
23/A
Banani Dhaka
Bangladesh
My dear Asik,
I hope you are keeping well. It has been quite a long time since we last met, and I often recall the pleasant days we spent together. Today I am writing to extend to you a warm invitation to visit my country, Bangladesh.
Bangladesh is a land of natural beauty, rich culture, and warm-hearted people. You will surely be delighted to see the lush green fields, wide rivers, and charming rural landscapes. The Sundarbans, the largest mangrove forest and home of the Royal Bengal Tiger, will certainly fascinate you. You may also like to visit Cox’s Bazar, the world’s longest natural sea beach, where the blue waves and golden sands create a heavenly atmosphere.
Apart from natural beauty, our country has many historical and cultural places such as the Lalbagh Fort, Ahsan Manzil, and the National Museum. You will also enjoy the colorful fairs, traditional music, and delicious foods like biryani, hilsa fish, and sweets.
Moreover, I strongly believe that the hospitality of our people will touch your heart. My family is eagerly waiting to welcome you, and we will make your stay here as comfortable and enjoyable as possible.
So please make up your mind and let me know when you will be able to come. Your visit will not only give me immense joy but also create unforgettable memories for both of us.
With best wishes and love,
Yours ever,
Anika

To: educationofficer@gmail.com
Subject: Invitation to Attend the Prize Giving Ceremony
Dear Sir,
I hope this email finds you in good health. On behalf of the students and teachers of Viqarunnisa Noon School and College, I am very pleased to inform you that our annual Prize Giving Ceremony will be held on 25 September 2025 in the school auditorium.
It will be a great honor for us if you kindly attend the program as our chief guest. Your presence will inspire our students to work harder and achieve greater success in their academic and co-curricular activities. The ceremony will include cultural performances, prize distribution, and speeches highlighting the achievements of our students.
We sincerely request you to grace the occasion with your valuable presence and encourage our learners.
We look forward to your kind response.
Sincerely yours,
Anik
On behalf of the students of
Viqarunnisa noon school and college,

cq_num: An email for sending money
text:
To: father@gmail.com
Subject: Request for Financial Support to Buy Books
Dear Father,
I hope this email finds you in good health and sound spirits. I am also doing well and continuing my studies with sincerity. You will be glad to know that our new academic year has recently begun, and the teachers have already started their lessons. For this purpose, I need to purchase a set of new textbooks, notebooks, and other necessary study materials.
Without these books, it is becoming quite difficult for me to follow the classes properly. As you are well aware, books are the most essential companions of a student, and timely collection of them is very important for smooth progress in study. Therefore, I earnestly request you to send me the required money at your earliest convenience so that I may buy the necessary books without any delay.
Please do not worry about me. I am studying regularly and determined to do well in the upcoming examinations. Convey my best regards to mother and my affection to younger sister.
Your loving son,
Rafiq

For this purpose, I need to purchase a set of new textbooks, notebooks, and other necessary study materials.
Without these books, it is becoming quite difficult for me to follow the classes properly. As you are well aware, books are the most essential companions of a student, and timely collection of them is very important for smooth progress in study. Therefore, I earnestly request you to send me the required money at your earliest convenience so that I may buy the necessary books without any delay.
Please do not worry about me. I am studying regularly and determined to do well in the upcoming examinations. Convey my best regards to mother and my affection to younger sister.
Your loving son,
Rafiq

cq_num: Inviting your friend to spend to the summer vacation.
text:
23rd September, 2025
Dhaka, Bangladesh
Dear Rahim,
I hope this letter finds you in the best of health and spirits. It has been a long time since we last met, and I have been missing our friendly chats and fun times at school. As the summer vacation is approaching, I thought it would be a wonderful idea if you could come and spend your holidays with me.
Our family is planning some interesting activities this summer, and I am sure you will enjoy them a lot. We have a small garden where we can play cricket and football. I am also planning to take you to the nearby river for boating and fishing, which I know you love. Moreover, my parents are very excited about having you here and they will prepare some delicious meals for us.
Apart from fun activities, we can also use this vacation to study together and prepare for the upcoming exams. You know, studying with a friend always makes learning more enjoyable. We can also watch some educational and entertaining programs and read books together in the evenings.
I sincerely hope that you will be able to accept my invitation. It would be really delightful to spend the summer vacation with you, creating beautiful memories that we will cherish for a long time. Please let me know as soon as possible so that I can make all the necessary arrangements.
Looking forward to your positive reply.
Yours affectionately,

cq_num: The Pied Piper of Hamelin
text:
The town of Hamelin in Germany was once troubled by a severe rat infestation. The rats were everywhere—inside homes, in the streets, and even in food stores. The people were desperate and didn’t know how to get rid of them.
One day, a strange man wearing a colorful coat came to the town. He called himself the Pied Piper and offered to remove all the rats in exchange for a thousand gold coins. The mayor and townspeople agreed.
The Pied Piper took out a magical pipe and played a special tune. To everyone’s surprise, the rats came out from every corner and followed him. He led them to the River Weser, where they drowned.
After the rats were gone, the Piper returned to collect his reward. However, the mayor broke his promise and refused to pay the full amount. Angry and disappointed, the Piper left.
The next day, the Piper came back and played another tune—this time, a sweet and cheerful one. All the children of Hamelin followed him, dancing and laughing. He led them into a mountain, which magically closed behind them. Only one child, who was lame and couldn't keep up, remained behind.
The people of Hamelin were heartbroken. They had lost all their children because of their dishonesty. Since then, the story has served as a warning to always keep one’s promises.
Moral: We should always keep our promises and act honestly.

cq_num: Honesty is the Best Policy
text:
Once upon a time, there lived a poor woodcutter named Atiq. He was an honest and hardworking man. Every day, Atiq would go deep into the forest to cut wood. He sold the wood in the nearby market to earn money for his family. Although he was poor, he never lied or cheated anyone.
One sunny morning, Atiq went to the forest as usual. While cutting wood near a river, his axe suddenly slipped from his hand and fell into the deep water. The river was very wide and full of strong currents. Atiq looked into the water, but he could not see his axe. He became very upset, as it was the only tool he had to earn a living.
Atiq sat by the riverbank and started to pray for help. Just then, a beautiful fairy appeared from the river. She asked, “Why are you so sad, kind man?” Atiq stood up and replied, “Dear fairy, my only axe has fallen into the river. Without it, I cannot cut wood or feed my family.”
The fairy smiled and said, “Don’t worry. I will help you.” She dived into the river and soon came out with a golden axe. She asked Atiq, “Is this your axe?” Atiq looked at it and said honestly, “No, this is not mine. My axe was not made of gold.”
The fairy went into the water again and returned with a silver axe. She asked again, “Is this one yours?” Atiq shook his head and replied, “No, this is also not mine.”
Finally, the fairy brought out an iron axe. Atiq’s face lit up with joy. “Yes!” he said, “This is my axe!”
The fairy was very pleased with Atiq’s honesty. She said, “You are a good and honest man. As a reward for your truthfulness, I will give you all three axes—the iron one, the silver one, and the golden one.” Atiq was surprised and thanked the fairy with a grateful heart.
From that day on, Atiq became well-known in his village for his honesty. He continued to live a simple life, but he was never poor again. Everyone respected him and learned from his example.
Moral: Honesty is always rewarded.

cq_num: The price of greed
text:
Once upon a time, a poor farmer lived in a small village with his wife. One day, he discovered something unusual in the nest of his goose—a shining, solid golden egg. At first, he thought it was a trick, but when it happened again the next day, he realized that the goose was truly special.
Every morning, the goose laid one golden egg. The farmer and his wife sold the eggs and slowly became very rich. They built a bigger house, bought new clothes, and lived a comfortable life. However, as their wealth increased, so did their greed. They were no longer satisfied with one egg a day.
The wife said to her husband, “Why should we wait every day for one egg? The goose must have a lot of gold inside her. Let’s cut her open and take it all at once.” Blinded by greed, the farmer agreed.
They killed the goose, hoping to find a great treasure inside. But to their shock, there was no gold inside—just like any ordinary goose. Their source of wealth was gone forever.
The couple regretted their foolish decision, but it was too late. Their greed had cost them everything. They soon became poor again and lived in sorrow for the rest of their lives.
Moral: Greed always leads to destruction.

cq_num: Failures are the pillars of success
text:
Many years ago, there lived a king of Scotland named Robert Bruce. He was a brave and patriotic ruler. He loved his country deeply and wanted to free it from the hands of the English. He fought many battles to gain independence for his people. But unfortunately, he was defeated six times in the battlefield. Each time, his soldiers lost courage and deserted him. Heartbroken and hopeless, the king hid himself in a lonely cave.
One rainy day, lying on the floor of the cave, the king was lost in deep thought. Suddenly, he
noticed a little spider trying to climb up the roof of the cave. The spider tried once but fell down. It tried again and again, but failed each time. Yet it did not lose heart. At last, on its seventh attempt, the spider succeeded in climbing up to the roof.
This small incident gave the king a great lesson. He thought, “If a tiny spider can succeed after repeated failures, why should I, a man and a king, lose courage?” His heart filled with new hope and strength. He came out of the cave, gathered his soldiers again, and inspired them with fresh determination. They fought bravely under his leadership. This time, fortune favored him. King Bruce defeated the English army and regained the freedom of his country.
The story of King Bruce and the spider teaches us a valuable lesson. Failure is not the end of life. Every failure gives us an opportunity to correct our mistakes and prepare better for the next attempt. Success comes only to those who keep trying with patience and courage. Just as King Bruce learned from the spider, we too must remember that failure is the pillar of success.
Moral : “Perseverance and patience lead to victory.

cq_num: The Two Rats and the Monkey
text:
Once upon a time, two hungry cats lived in a village. One day, they found a piece of bread on the roadside. Both cats jumped at it at the same time and caught hold of it. Each claimed that the bread belonged to him. They began to quarrel loudly.
One cat said, “I found it first, so it is mine.”
The other argued, “But I touched it first, so it belongs to me.”
As they could not settle the matter, they thought of dividing the bread equally. But neither trusted the other to do the division fairly. While they were still quarreling, a clever monkey happened to pass by. The monkey was very cunning and greedy. He thought to himself, “This is a good chance for me to have a nice meal. I will trick these foolish cats.”
The monkey came forward and said, “My dear friends, why are you quarreling? Let me help you. I am wise and fair, so I will divide the bread equally.” The cats, being simple, agreed and handed the bread to him.
The monkey tore the bread into two pieces, but one piece was slightly larger than the other. He frowned and said, “Oh, this is not fair. Let me take a bite from the bigger piece to make them equal.” Saying this, he ate a portion of the larger piece. But now the other piece looked bigger. So the monkey took a bite from it too.
He continued biting alternately from both pieces, pretending to make them equal. Each time he ate, he shook his head and said, “Still not equal. Let me try again.” In this way, the monkey went on eating until almost the whole bread was finished. Finally, only two small crumbs were left. The monkey gave one crumb to each cat and said, “Now the bread is divided equally. Farewell, my friends.”
The two cats looked at each other in despair. They were both still hungry and realized they had been fooled by the clever monkey. They had lost their bread because of their foolish quarrel and lack of trust.
From that day on, the cats learned a bitter lesson: selfish quarrels only invite others to take advantage, and unity and fairness are always better than fighting over small gains.
Moral: When we quarrel among ourselves, others take advantage.

cq_num: The Liar Cowboy / Shepherd
text:
Once there was a cowboy named Arif who lived on the outskirts of a small village. He was well-known among the villagers, but not for a good reason. Arif loved to tell lies and exaggerate every small thing that happened to him. One day he rode into the village shouting, “Help! Wild wolves are attacking my cattle!” Hearing this, the villagers ran to help him with sticks and tools. But when they reached his farm, they found everything safe. Arif laughed and said, “I just wanted to see how fast you could run!”
The villagers were angry but forgave him. A few weeks later, Arif shouted again, “Fire! My barn is burning!” Once more, the villagers ran to help, only to find it was another lie. Arif laughed again, thinking it was fun to trick everyone. Slowly, people stopped believing him.
One evening, while Arif was out on the plain, a real danger appeared. A pack of wolves came suddenly and attacked his herd. Arif ran into the village shouting, “Help! Wolves are really attacking now!” But the villagers, remembering all his lies, ignored his cries. They thought it was another joke.
Arif tried to save the cows himself, but he could not fight the wolves alone. Many of his cattle were lost, and he got badly injured. Lying had cost him dearly. The next day, the villagers helped him finally, but by then, the damage was done. Arif realized the hard truth: no one trusts a liar, even when he tells the truth.
From that day, Arif changed completely. He never lied again. He worked honestly, helped others, and slowly regained the trust of the villagers. Everyone respected him because he learned that honesty is far more valuable than tricks and false stories.
Moral: “A liar is never trusted, even when he tells the truth.

cq_num: The Lion and the Mouse
text:
Once upon a time, in a dense forest, there lived a mighty lion. He was the king of the jungle, feared by all animals. One afternoon, after a big meal, the lion lay under a shady tree and fell asleep.
While he was sleeping, a tiny mouse came out of its hole and started playing near the lion. It ran up and down the lion’s back, tickling his ears and tail. Suddenly, the lion woke up with a loud roar. He caught the mouse under his big paw.
“How dare you wake me up?” the lion growled. “I will eat you for this!”
The little mouse trembled in fear and begged, “Please, mighty lion, let me go. I didn’t mean to disturb you. If you spare my life, I may be able to help you one day!”
The lion laughed. “You? A tiny mouse? Help me? That’s the funniest thing I’ve ever heard.” But he felt pity for the little creature and decided to let him go.
A few days later, the lion was wandering through the forest when he got caught in a hunter’s net. No matter how hard he tried, he couldn’t escape. He roared loudly for help.
Hearing the lion’s roar, the mouse came running. He saw the lion trapped and said, “Don’t worry, I’ll help you.”
The mouse began to gnaw at the ropes with his sharp teeth. Slowly and steadily, he chewed through the net until the lion was free.
The lion looked at the mouse with great gratitude. “Thank you, little friend. I never thought you could help me, but you saved my life!”
The mouse smiled and replied, “Even the smallest friend can make a big difference.”
Moral of the story: No act of kindness is ever wasted. / Even the smallest creature can be of great help.

cq_num: Bayzid’s Devotion to His Mother
text:
Long ago, in a quiet village surrounded by green fields and tall trees, there lived a kind and obedient boy named Bayzid. He was known for his gentle nature, love of learning, and above all, his deep respect and love for his mother.
One night, when Bayzid was studying under the dim light of a lamp, he suddenly heard his mother calling from her bed. Her voice was weak, and she asked, “Bayzid, I feel very thirsty. Could you bring me some water?”
Bayzid immediately got up and went to fetch water. But when he reached the well, he found that the rope used to draw water was broken. The only other well was quite far from their house. Still, Bayzid didn’t hesitate. He ran through the darkness, barefoot, to fetch water from the distant well.
It took him a long time, but finally, he returned with a pot of water. However, when he reached home, he saw that his mother had fallen asleep. Tired but devoted, Bayzid didn’t wake her. Instead, he stood beside her bed, holding the water pot in his hand, waiting patiently for her to wake up.
Hours passed. Night turned into morning. Bayzid stood silently the whole time, without moving, without disturbing his mother’s sleep.
When his mother finally woke up and saw her son still standing with the water, tears filled her eyes. She was deeply touched by Bayzid’s love and devotion. She raised her hands and prayed, “O Allah, bless my son with great wisdom and honor.”
That sincere prayer from a mother’s heart was answered. Bayzid grew up to become a wise and respected man, known as Bayzid Bostami, a great saint of Islam. People still remember him for his deep love for his mother and his devotion to God.
Moral: Obeying and respecting one’s parents, especially the mother, brings blessings and honor in this world and the next.

cq_num: The Thirsty Crow
text:
One a hot summer afternoon, the sun was shining brightly in the sky. The trees stood still in the heat, and the ground was dry and cracked. All the ponds and streams had dried up, and not a drop of water could be seen anywhere.
High up in the sky, a black crow was flying, looking here and there. He had been searching for water for a long time. His wings were tired, and his throat was dry. "Caw… caw…" he cried weakly, hoping to find something to drink.
Suddenly, the crow saw something shiny below. He quickly flew down and landed near a garden. There, under a shady tree, he saw a clay pot. The crow hopped closer and peeked inside. To his delight, he saw some water at the bottom of the pot!
But when he tried to drink it, he realized he couldn’t reach the water. The neck of the pot was too narrow, and the water level was too low. The crow looked around helplessly. He was very thirsty, but giving up was not an option.
Just then, an idea struck his clever mind. He looked around and found small pebbles lying on the ground. One by one, the crow picked up the stones in his beak and dropped them into the pot.
He kept dropping more and more pebbles into the pot. Slowly but surely, the water level began to rise. The crow kept working patiently. After several tries, the water finally rose high enough for him to drink.
With great joy, the thirsty crow dipped his beak into the cool water and drank deeply. He felt fresh and full of energy again. Flapping his wings happily, he flew away into the sky, proud of his clever thinking.
Moral of the Story:
"Where there is a will, there is a way."
Or: "Think smart and never give up!"

cq_num: Who will bell the cat?
text:
Once upon a time in a cozy barn nestled in a peaceful village, there lived a colony of mice. The mice were cheerful and lively, enjoying the grains stored in the barn. Life was good—until a new cat arrived.
The cat, a fierce and silent hunter named Whiskers, began catching the mice one by one. Fear spread through the colony like wildfire. The once-happy mice were now too scared to even leave their holes.
One evening, the mice held a meeting. “We must do something!” cried an old gray mouse. “If this goes on, none of us will be left!”
A young, clever mouse named Tiptoes stood up. “I have an idea,” he said. “Let’s tie a bell around the cat’s neck. That way, we’ll always hear it coming and have time to escape.”
The mice squeaked with excitement. “Brilliant! That will save us!”
But then, the elder mouse raised his paw. “Yes, but who is to bell the cat?”
Suddenly, the barn fell silent. All the mice looked at each other. No one volunteered. The idea sounded perfect—until it came time to actually do it.
One by one, the mice made excuses. “I’m too old,” said one. “I’m too small,” said another. “I have a bad leg,” another added. In the end, the meeting ended without a solution.
From that day on, the mice remained cautious and alert—but they never did bell the cat.
Moral: It's easy to suggest daring plans, but someone must have the courage to carry them out.

cq_num:
A story on king Midas
King Midas and the Golden Touch
text:
Long ago, in the land of Phrygia, there lived a wealthy king named Midas. He had everything he could ever want—gold, jewels, and a grand palace. But King Midas was not satisfied. He loved gold more than anything else in the world and wished he had even more.
One day, Midas did a kind deed for the god Dionysus. In return, the god offered to grant him one wish.
Without thinking carefully, King Midas said, “I wish that everything I touch turns to gold!”
Dionysus warned him, “Are you sure? This wish may not bring you the happiness you expect.”
But Midas insisted, so the god granted his wish.
At first, Midas was thrilled. He touched a stone—it turned to gold. He touched a tree—it turned to gold. Soon, everything in his garden sparkled. He was overjoyed.
But when he sat down to eat, his food turned to gold. When he tried to drink water, it became gold as well. He began to panic.
Then, to his horror, his young daughter ran to hug him—and she turned into a golden statue.
Heartbroken and full of regret, Midas begged Dionysus to take back the gift.
Moved by the king’s sorrow, Dionysus told him to wash in the river Pactolus to remove the curse. Midas rushed to the river, and as he bathed, the golden touch disappeared. His daughter returned to life, and everything became normal again.
From that day on, King Midas learned to value love, kindness, and the simple joys of life more than riches. He became a wiser and humbler king.
Moral of the Story: Greed brings destruction.

cq_num: A Fox Without a Tail
text:
Once upon a time, in a green forest, there lived a clever fox. One day, while running fast through the bushes, his tail got caught in a hunter’s trap. He struggled hard to free himself. At last, he managed to escape, but his beautiful tail was cut off.
The fox felt very sad and ashamed. He thought to himself, “How ugly I look without my tail! All the other animals will laugh at me.” He did not want anyone to make fun of him. Then he made a plan.
The next day, the fox called a meeting of all the foxes in the forest. When they gathered, he stood in front of them and said, “Friends, listen to me. I have found that a tail is useless. It is only a burden. We do not need it at all. Without a tail, we can run faster and feel lighter. I suggest that all of you cut off your tails like me. It will make life easier and more comfortable.”
Some foxes began to think about his words. But an old and wise fox stood up and said, “My friend, you have lost your tail in an accident, and now you want us to lose ours too. You are only saying this because you are feeling ashamed of your own condition. Do not try to make us suffer just to hide your own shame.”
Hearing this, all the foxes understood the truth. They laughed at the tailless fox and left the meeting. The fox felt more embarrassed than before. He realized that lying and giving bad advice only made his situation worse.
From that day, the fox kept quiet and accepted his appearance. He learned that it is better to accept reality rather than deceive others.
Moral: Do not try to mislead others to hide your own faults.

cq_num: Dress Does Not Make a Man Great
text:
Sheikh Saadi, the great Persian poet and philosopher, was once invited to the court of a king. The king had arranged a grand feast and many noblemen, scholars, and courtiers were present. Saadi, as usual, wore very simple clothes, for he never cared about outward appearance.
When he entered the palace, the guards and attendants did not recognize him. Seeing his plain dress, they thought he was an ordinary man. They did not show him proper respect and asked him to sit in a corner. Even the courtiers looked at him with contempt. Saadi noticed everything but remained calm.
After a while, he quietly went home and returned wearing a rich, gorgeous dress. This time, the guards saluted him respectfully and led him to the seat of honor near the king. Delicious food was served in front of him and everyone seemed eager to please him.
Then Sheikh Saadi began to do something unusual. He started putting pieces of food into the pockets of his fine dress. Everyone was astonished. The king, unable to hide his curiosity, asked politely, “Respected Sheikh, why are you feeding your dress instead of eating yourself?”
Saadi smiled and replied, “Your Majesty, when I came here in my simple dress, nobody cared for me. But when I wore this costly dress, everyone honored me. It seems the respect is not for me, but for my dress. Therefore, the food should go to the dress.”
Hearing this, the king and his courtiers felt ashamed of their shallow attitude. They realized that true honor and greatness do not lie in dress or wealth but in wisdom, virtue, and character.
From that day, the king treated Sheikh Saadi with the deepest respect, and the courtiers also learned a valuable lesson.
Moral: Dress does not make a man great; greatness depends on knowledge, character, and good deeds.

cq_num: An Honest Woodcutter
text:
Once upon a time, there lived a poor woodcutter in a small village near a forest. He earned his living by cutting wood from the forest and selling it in the market. Though he was poor, he was very honest and hardworking. He never desired anything that did not belong to him.
One day, while cutting a tree beside a river, his axe suddenly slipped from his hand and fell into the deep water. The woodcutter became sad and worried because the axe was his only means of livelihood. Without it, he could not earn bread for his family. He sat on the bank and began to weep.
Hearing his cries, the river-god appeared before him. The god asked, “Why are you crying, my son?” The woodcutter told him the whole story. The god felt pity for the poor man and dived into the river. After a while, he came out with a golden axe and asked, “Is this your axe?” The woodcutter looked at it but shook his head, saying, “No, sir, that is not mine. My axe was made of iron.”
The god dived into the water again and returned with a silver axe. “Perhaps this is yours?” he asked. The woodcutter again said with honesty, “No, sir, that is not mine either.” Finally, the god brought out a simple iron axe. The woodcutter’s eyes shone with joy. “Yes, sir, this one is mine,” he said happily.
The god was very pleased with his honesty. He said, “You are a truthful man. As a reward, I give you not only your own axe but also the golden and silver axes.” Saying this, he handed over all three axes to the woodcutter. The poor man was overjoyed and thanked the god with all his heart.
The news of this incident spread through the village, and everyone praised the woodcutter for his honesty. The villagers learned that honesty always brings rewards, while dishonesty may bring temporary gain but leads to sorrow in the end.
Moral: Honesty is the best policy.

cq_num: Play to One May Cause Death to Another
text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet village, there was a large pond at the edge of the fields. The pond was full of clear water, green lilies, and many frogs. The frogs spent their days happily croaking, swimming, and leaping from one lily pad to another. For them, the pond was both a playground and a home.
Near the pond lived a group of naughty boys. They were mischievous by nature and always searched for something to laugh at. One summer afternoon, when the sun was bright and the fields were empty, the boys gathered near the pond. They began to notice the frogs jumping in and out of the water. At first, they watched with curiosity, but soon their wicked minds started to plan a cruel game.
One of the boys picked up a stone and threw it at the frogs. The stone splashed into the water, and the frogs quickly dived into the pond in fear. The boy laughed loudly, and the others clapped their hands with joy. Then, each of them began throwing stones one after another, finding great amusement in watching the frightened frogs leap for safety. To them, it was only harmless play.
But the frogs were in real danger. Many of the stones hit their fragile bodies. Some were struck on the head, some on the back, and some even died instantly. The pond, once a happy home, became a place of terror. The older frogs tried to hide in the deeper water, while the younger ones cried out helplessly.
At last, one brave old frog rose to the surface. He looked at the boys with sad eyes and spoke in a sorrowful voice, “Dear boys, why do you find joy in hurting us? To you, it is only a game, but to us, it is a matter of life and death. What seems like play to you is death to us.”
The boys were stunned. They had never thought about the suffering of the frogs. Shame covered their faces, and their laughter turned to silence. Slowly, they dropped the stones from their hands and walked away, realizing the cruelty of their actions. From that day onward, they never disturbed the pond again.
The frogs, though wounded and scared, found peace once more. The pond regained its calmness, but the boys carried in their hearts the unforgettable lesson they had learned.
Moral: What seems pleasure to one may cause sorrow to another.

cq_num: A Rickshaw Puller and a Lottery Ticket
text:
Rahim was a poor rickshaw puller who lived in Dhaka city. His life was nothing but a tale of endless hardship. From dawn till late at night, he pulled his rickshaw through the crowded and noisy streets. Whatever little he earned was hardly enough to feed his small family. He lived in a shabby hut in a slum with his wife and two children. Every evening, when he returned home with his tired body and empty pockets, he often wondered how long he could continue this struggle. Yet, Rahim was an honest man. He never cheated his passengers and always tried to live with dignity.
One afternoon, while waiting for passengers near a bus stand, Rahim saw a man loudly selling lottery tickets. At first, Rahim laughed at the idea. “How can such a piece of paper change my life?” he thought. But the seller kept calling, “Who knows? Maybe today is your lucky day! Only five taka for a chance at fortune!” Rahim had a few coins left after buying some rice for the family. Though hesitant, he felt a strange hope in his heart. After much thought, he bought a ticket, folded it carefully, and kept it in his pocket.
Days went by. Rahim almost forgot about the ticket because he was too busy with his daily struggle. He continued pulling his rickshaw, sweating in the scorching sun, getting wet in the rain, and earning just enough for survival. But deep inside, a little spark of hope remained in his heart.
One evening, Rahim was having his simple supper of rice and lentils when one of his neighbours rushed to his hut, holding a newspaper. “Rahim bhai, have you checked the lottery results? A ticket sold in our area has won the first prize!” the neighbour said excitedly. Rahim’s heart started beating fast. He quickly searched for the ticket and matched the number with the paper. To his great surprise and joy, it was his ticket! He had actually won the first prize.
Rahim could not believe his eyes. Tears rolled down his cheeks. His wife and children cried with happiness. At last, fortune had smiled upon this poor rickshaw puller. With the prize money, Rahim built a small but comfortable house. He admitted his children into a good school, started a small grocery shop, and gave up pulling rickshaws. However, he did not become proud or selfish. He always remembered the painful days of poverty. That is why he helped other poor rickshaw pullers, gave food to hungry children, and stood beside people in need.
The story of Rahim teaches us an important lesson. Life is uncertain, and fortune may change suddenly. But at the same time, we should not depend only on luck. Hard work, honesty, and patience are the real wealth of a man. Luck may come once, but good character remains forever.
Moral: Life is uncertain, and fortune may come at any time.

cq_num: The Hare and the Tortoise
text:
Once upon a time, in a green forest, there lived a hare who was very proud of his speed. He could run like the wind, leap across bushes, and dash past any animal in a blink of an eye. Because of this, he often boasted to others, “No one in this forest can ever beat me in a race. I am the fastest of all!”
The animals grew tired of hearing his constant bragging. Among them was a wise and humble tortoise. He was slow by nature, but he believed in patience and steady effort. One day, to teach the hare a lesson, the tortoise calmly said, “Hare, I may be slow, but I challenge you to a race.”
The forest burst into laughter. The hare laughed louder than anyone else. “You? Race with me? You must be joking!” he mocked. But the tortoise remained firm. At last, the hare agreed, thinking it would be a chance to prove his speed once more.
The next morning, all the animals gathered to watch. The racecourse was marked—from the big oak tree to the hill by the river. At the signal, the race began.
The hare darted off like an arrow. Within moments, he was far ahead. He turned back and saw the tortoise moving slowly, step by step. “Ha! He will never catch me,” thought the hare. Confident of victory, he decided to rest under a shady tree. “I’ll take a nap and still reach the finish line before him,” he said. Soon, he was fast asleep.
Meanwhile, the tortoise never stopped. Though his legs were heavy and his pace was slow, he kept moving forward with determination. Step after step, he passed the tree where the hare lay snoring. The animals cheered for him, amazed at his patience.
Hours later, the hare suddenly woke up. The sun was already setting. Alarmed, he sprang up and dashed toward the finish line. But it was too late. The tortoise was already crossing the line, cheered by the crowd.
The hare was shocked and ashamed. He hung his head and admitted, “I was careless and proud. You have won fairly.”
The tortoise smiled and replied, “Slow and steady wins the race.”
Moral of the story: consistency leads to success.

Story writing STORY The Hare and the Tortoise The Hare and the Tortoise The Hare and the Tortoise Once upon a time, in a green forest, there lived a hare who was very proud of his speed. He could run like the wind, leap across bushes, and dash past any animal in a blink of an eye. Because of this, he often boasted to others, “No one in this forest can ever beat me in a race. I am the fastest of all!”
The animals grew tired of hearing his constant bragging. Among them was a wise and humble tortoise. He was slow by nature, but he believed in patience and steady effort. One day, to teach the hare a lesson, the tortoise calmly said, “Hare, I may be slow, but I challenge you to a race.”
The forest burst into laughter. The hare laughed louder than anyone else. “You? Race with me? You must be joking!” he mocked. But the tortoise remained firm. At last, the hare agreed, thinking it would be a chance to prove his speed once more.
The next morning, all the animals gathered to watch. The racecourse was marked—from the big oak tree to the hill by the river. At the signal, the race began.
The hare darted off like an arrow. Within moments, he was far ahead. He turned back and saw the tortoise moving slowly, step by step. “Ha! He will never catch me,” thought the hare. Confident of victory, he decided to rest under a shady tree. “I’ll take a nap and still reach the finish line before him,” he said. Soon, he was fast asleep.
Meanwhile, the tortoise never stopped. Though his legs were heavy and his pace was slow, he kept moving forward with determination. Step after step, he passed the tree where the hare lay snoring. The animals cheered for him, amazed at his patience.
Hours later, the hare suddenly woke up. The sun was already setting. Alarmed, he sprang up and dashed toward the finish line. But it was too late. The tortoise was already crossing the line, cheered by the crowd.
The hare was shocked and ashamed. He hung his head and admitted, “I was careless and proud. You have won fairly.”
The tortoise smiled and replied, “Slow and steady wins the race.”
Moral of the story: consistency leads to success.

cq_num: Unity is Strength
text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet village, there lived an old farmer with four sons. The sons were strong and hardworking, but unfortunately, they never agreed with one another. They quarreled over the smallest matters—whether it was dividing food, working in the fields, or even playing games. Day by day, their constant fighting made the father very worried.
The farmer often advised them, saying, “My dear sons, if you keep quarreling, you will bring misery upon yourselves. You must live in unity, for strength lies in togetherness.” But the sons never paid attention to their father’s words.
One day, the old farmer thought of a plan. He called his sons and said, “My dear children, I have grown old and weak. Before I die, I want to test your strength. Today you must prove how strong you are.”
The sons became curious. The farmer then brought a bundle of strong sticks tied together with a rope. Handing the bundle to his eldest son, he said, “Try and break this.” The young man tried with all his strength, but the bundle did not break. Then the second son tried, then the third, and finally the youngest. Each of them pulled, twisted, and pressed the sticks with all their might, but the bundle remained unbroken.
The farmer smiled gently and said, “Now untie the bundle and take one stick each.” The sons did as their father said. Then the farmer asked them to break the sticks individually. This time, with little effort, they snapped their sticks into pieces.
The old farmer looked at them and said in a serious tone, “My sons, do you see the truth now? When the sticks were bound together, none of you could break them. But when they were separated, each stick was broken easily. In the same way, if you stand united, no enemy will ever harm you. But if you remain divided and keep quarreling, you will become weak, and others will easily defeat you.”
The sons realized their mistake. They felt ashamed of their constant quarrels and promised their father that they would live together in peace and harmony. From that day, they worked hand in hand in the fields, helped one another in need, and never quarreled again. The old farmer felt greatly relieved and blessed his sons.
Moral: True strength lies not in individual power but in unity and cooperation.

cq_num: The Thirsty Crow
text:
One hot summer day, a thirsty crow was flying over the fields in search of water. The sun was burning, and the crow felt very weak. It had been flying for hours but could not find a single drop of water. At last, it saw a small pitcher under a tree. The crow was very happy and immediately flew down to quench its thirst.
The crow looked into the pitcher and saw some water at the bottom. But the water level was too low, and the crow’s beak could not reach it. The crow tried again and again, but it was of no use. Feeling tired and frustrated, the crow sat on the edge of the pitcher to think.
After a while, the crow remembered something it had seen before. It started picking small pebbles from the ground and dropping them carefully into the pitcher, one by one. Slowly, the water level began to rise. The crow kept dropping the pebbles until the water came near the top.
Finally, the clever crow drank the water and satisfied its thirst. Feeling happy and refreshed, it flew away, thinking about how patience and intelligence had helped it survive.
This story teaches an important lesson: when faced with a problem, we should think wisely and find a solution instead of giving up. The crow’s determination and cleverness remind us that “Where there is a will, there is a way.”
Moral: Determination and intelligence are the keys to success.

cq_num: Slow and Steady Wins the Race
text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet forest, there lived many animals of different kinds. Among them were a proud hare and a humble tortoise. The hare was famous for his speed. He could run faster than any other animal, and he often boasted of this talent. The tortoise, on the other hand, was very slow but hardworking and determined.
One sunny morning, the hare began to tease the tortoise. He laughed loudly and said, “You are the slowest creature in this forest! I can run ten times faster than you before you even take a few steps.” The tortoise felt humiliated, but he calmly replied, “Dear hare, you may be quick, but I believe that steady effort can also bring success. Why don’t we test it? Let us have a race.”
Hearing this, the hare burst out laughing. “A race between you and me? That is ridiculous! But very well, I accept your challenge. I will show everyone how foolish you are.” Soon, the news spread all over the forest, and many animals gathered to see the unusual contest.
The race began. As expected, the hare ran swiftly and was soon far ahead of the tortoise. Looking back, he saw the tortoise moving slowly, step by step, far behind him. Confident of his victory, the hare thought, “This race is already mine. The tortoise can never catch me. I have plenty of time to rest.” So he lay down under a shady tree and quickly fell asleep.
Meanwhile, the tortoise did not stop. He moved steadily, one step after another, without looking back or losing courage. Hours passed, and while the hare was sleeping peacefully, the tortoise kept moving forward. Finally, he reached the finish line where all the animals were waiting. Everyone cheered loudly for the tortoise.
Suddenly, the hare woke up. He ran as fast as he could toward the end of the race. But alas! It was too late. When he arrived, he saw the tortoise had already crossed the line and was being praised for his patience and determination. The hare hung his head in shame.
The forest animals learned an important lesson that day. Overconfidence and laziness can bring failure, while perseverance and steady effort lead to true success.
Moral “Consistency is more powerful than speed.”

cq_num: A Friend in Need is a Friend Indeed
text:
Once upon a time, there lived two close friends named Asif and Salman in a small village. They were very good friends and always boasted about their strong bond. They promised to help each other in all situations, whether good or bad. One day, they decided to go to a nearby town through a dense forest. Before entering the forest, they promised once again to stand by each other in times of danger.
As they were walking through the forest, they talked and laughed happily. Suddenly, a wild bear appeared before them. Both were frightened. Asif, who knew how to climb trees, at once ran up a nearby tree to save himself. But poor Salman did not know how to climb. He was terrified and lay flat on the ground, holding his breath, pretending to be dead.
The bear came close to Salman and sniffed him all over. Thinking that he was dead, the bear slowly went away, as bears never touch a dead body. After the bear disappeared into the forest, Asif climbed down the tree. He smiled and asked Salman jokingly, “What did the bear whisper into your ear?”
Salman replied calmly, “The bear told me not to trust a friend who leaves you in danger.” Hearing this, Asif felt ashamed and realized his mistake. He understood that a true friend never abandons his companion in times of trouble.
From that day, Asif decided to be a better and more loyal friend. Salman forgave him, and they continued their friendship with mutual trust and sincerity. The incident taught them both an important lesson — that friendship is not just about sharing joy, but also about standing by each other in danger and distress.
Moral: A friend in need is a friend indeed

cq_num: The Ready Wit of a Boy
text:
Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a brave and intelligent boy named Asif. He was known for his courage and quick thinking. One afternoon, while walking home from school, he noticed that the train to the neighboring town was moving unusually fast. As Asif approached the railway crossing, he saw a group of children playing dangerously close to the tracks. To his horror, a small cart got stuck on the track ahead.
Asif realized that if the train continued, it could lead to a terrible accident. Without wasting a second, he ran towards the signal post and tried to warn the driver, but the driver was too far to hear him. Thinking quickly, Asif climbed onto the trackside signal lever and pulled it, hoping it would trigger the emergency brakes. Meanwhile, he shouted at the top of his voice to alert people nearby.
His quick actions caught the attention of a railway guard who was passing by. The guard rushed to the train and signaled the driver to stop. Slowly, the train came to a halt just a few meters from the stuck cart. The children were safe, and no one was hurt. The railway authorities arrived and praised Asif for his presence of mind and courage.
News of Asif’s bravery spread throughout the town. People admired how a young boy, with his intelligence and quick thinking, prevented a major disaster. Asif humbly told everyone that he had only done what any responsible person should do to save lives.
From that day on, Asif became a local hero. His story taught everyone an important lesson: quick thinking and courage can prevent accidents and save many lives.
Moral: Courage and quick thinking can avert disasters and save lives.

cq_num: বইমেলা
text:
বইমেলা জাতীয় জীবনে এক অনন্য সাধারণ মেলা যা বইকে কেন্দ্র করে লেখক ও পাঠকের মিলনমেলায় পরিণত হয়।
অত্যন্ত আনন্দঘন ও উৎসবমুখর পরিবেশে বই কেনা-বেচার সুযোগ ঘটে বইমেলায়।  পাঠক সেখান থেকে তাঁর পছন্দের বই দেখেশুনে কিনতে পারেন। জার্মানির ফ্রাঙ্কফুর্ট শহরে বিশ্বের বৃহত্তম বইমেলার আয়োজন করা হয়। বাংলাদেশে বইমেলার সূচনা হয় ১৯৭২ সালে মুক্তধারার প্রকাশক চিত্তরঞ্জন সাহার উদ্যোগে।  মেলায় শুধু বই কেনাবেচাই নয়, নানা ধরনের সাংস্কৃতিক ও একাডেমিক কর্মকাণ্ড আয়োজন করা হয় বইমেলায় । বাংলাদেশের মানুষের কাছে একুশে ফেব্রুয়ারি অত্যন্ত তাৎপর্যপূর্ণ একটি দিন। এক সাগর রক্তের বিনিময়ে অর্জিত মাতৃভাষাকে রক্ষার এই দিনটি বাঙালি সত্তার মর্মমূলে গাঁথা। দিনটি আমাদের সংগ্রামী চেতনা ও সংস্কৃতির অভিজ্ঞান। একুশের সংগ্রামী চেতনাকে স্মরণীয় করে রাখার জন্য ১৯৫২ সালের পর থেকেই সারা মাস জুড়ে নানা ধরনের আয়োজন করা হয়। একুশের মহান বইমেলা এ সকল আয়োজনের মধ্যে অন্যতম। স্বাধীনতা-উত্তর বাংলাদেশে ১৯৭২ সালের ৮ ফেব্রুয়ারি চিত্তরঞ্জন সাহা বর্তমান বাংলা একাডেমি প্রাঙ্গনের বটতলায় এক টুকরো চটের ওপর ৩২ খানা বই সাজিয়ে যে মেলার গোড়াপত্তন করেন তাতে বাংলা একাডেমি সম্পৃক্ত হয় ১৯৭৮ সালে। ১৯৮৩ সালে বাংলা একাডেমিতে অমর একুশে গ্রন্থমেলার প্রথম আয়োজন হয়। আজ বাংলা একাডেমি প্রাঙ্গণে স্থান সংকুলান না হওয়ায় ২০১৪ সাল থেকে বইমেলা সম্প্রসারিত করা হয় সোহরাওয়ার্দী উদ্যানে। পুরো ফেব্রুয়ারি মাস জুড়ে একুশে বইমেলাকে উপলক্ষ্য করে এই দেশের অগণিত ক্রেতা, দর্শক ও পাঠকের সমাবেশ ঘটে এবং একটা সাহিত্য-সাংস্কৃতিক পরিবেশের সৃষ্টি হয়। লেখক, সাহিত্যিক, সাংস্কৃতিককর্মী, রাজনৈতিক নেতাকর্মী, শিক্ষক, গবেষক, বুদ্ধিজীবী, সাংবাদিক-সহ নানা স্তরের মানুষের উপস্থিতিতে বইমেলা সরগরম হয়ে ওঠে। তবে তরুণ ছাত্রছাত্রীদের উপস্থিতিই বেশি। দূরদূরান্ত থেকে বহু প্রকাশক আসেন, আসে নানা ধরনের বই। অচেনা, অজানা অনেক বইয়ের সন্ধান মেলে। সব কেনা সম্ভব না হলেও অনেক বইয়ের সাথে পরিচয় ঘটে। প্রকাশক মেলা প্রাঙ্গণে ক্রেতাদের কাছে সরাসরি বই বিক্রির সুযোগ পান। সুযোগ পান পাঠকদের চাহিদা জানার। শত শত স্টলের মধ্য থেকে ক্রেতারা অভিরুচি অনুযায়ী স্টল নির্বাচন করে বই কিনতে পারেন। সীমাবদ্ধ প্রাঙ্গণে অসংখ্য রকম বইয়ের সমাবেশ ঘটায় ক্রেতারা স্বল্প আয়াসে পছন্দের বই সংগ্রহ করতে পারেন এবং প্রয়োজনীয় বইয়ের তালিকা সংগ্রহ করেন; যা পরবর্তীতে কাজে লাগে। একটি পাঠানুরাগী- প্রজন্ম গড়ে তুলতে এই মেলা অগ্রণী ভূমিকা পালন করে। মেলায় প্রতিদিন নতুন প্রকাশিত বই এসে যুক্ত হয়।  একুশের বইমেলায় পুরাতনের সাথে নতুন লেখকের মেলবন্ধন হয়। বহু নন্দিত কবি-সাহিত্যিক মেলায় এসে স্টলে বসেন, কৌতূহলী তরুণ বইপ্রেমিকদের সাথে কথা বলেন- অটোগ্রাফ দেন। লেখক-পাঠকের মহামিলন ঘটে এখানে। বইমেলার এক প্রান্তের মঞ্চে বিকেল-সন্ধ্যা মিলে চলে আলোচনা অনুষ্ঠান ও সাংস্কৃতিক অনুষ্ঠান যা দর্শকদের উপভোগ্য। অসংখ্য খাবার দোকানে চলে বন্ধুবান্ধবদের আড্ডা। তথ্যকেন্দ্র থেকে তথ্য দেওয়া হয় নতুন প্রকাশিত বইয়ের। মেলায় প্রবেশের সিংহতোরণটি থাকে দৃষ্টিনন্দিত ও সুসজ্জিত। একুশের বইমেলা আমাদের জাতীয় চেতনার দ্যোতক। এই মেলা আমাদের প্রাণ ও মনের মেলা। শত প্রাণের উদ্দীপ্ত উপস্থিতি, নতুন বইয়ের গন্ধে মৌ মৌ এই মেলা বাঙালির প্রেরণার উৎস। বইমেলা বই কেনা, পঠন-পাঠনের অভ্যাস ও ভাব বিনিময়ের মিলন তীর্থ।  যেকোনো বই মেলার গাম্ভীর্য, ভাব-ঐশ্বর্য ও ঐতিহ্য অক্ষুণ্ণ রাখার দায়িত্ব আমাদের সবার এবং এর মধ্য দিয়ে গড়ে উঠবে একটি শিক্ষিত ও সংস্কৃতিমনা বাঙালি জাতি।

অনুচ্ছেদ অন বইমেলা বইমেলা বইমেলা বইমেলা জাতীয় জীবনে এক অনন্য সাধারণ মেলা যা বইকে কেন্দ্র করে লেখক ও পাঠকের মিলনমেলায় পরিণত হয়।
অত্যন্ত আনন্দঘন ও উৎসবমুখর পরিবেশে বই কেনা-বেচার সুযোগ ঘটে বইমেলায়।  পাঠক সেখান থেকে তাঁর পছন্দের বই দেখেশুনে কিনতে পারেন। জার্মানির ফ্রাঙ্কফুর্ট শহরে বিশ্বের বৃহত্তম বইমেলার আয়োজন করা হয়। বাংলাদেশে বইমেলার সূচনা হয় ১৯৭২ সালে মুক্তধারার প্রকাশক চিত্তরঞ্জন সাহার উদ্যোগে।  মেলায় শুধু বই কেনাবেচাই নয়, নানা ধরনের সাংস্কৃতিক ও একাডেমিক কর্মকাণ্ড আয়োজন করা হয় বইমেলায় । বাংলাদেশের মানুষের কাছে একুশে ফেব্রুয়ারি অত্যন্ত তাৎপর্যপূর্ণ একটি দিন। এক সাগর রক্তের বিনিময়ে অর্জিত মাতৃভাষাকে রক্ষার এই দিনটি বাঙালি সত্তার মর্মমূলে গাঁথা। দিনটি আমাদের সংগ্রামী চেতনা ও সংস্কৃতির অভিজ্ঞান। একুশের সংগ্রামী চেতনাকে স্মরণীয় করে রাখার জন্য ১৯৫২ সালের পর থেকেই সারা মাস জুড়ে নানা ধরনের আয়োজন করা হয়। একুশের মহান বইমেলা এ সকল আয়োজনের মধ্যে অন্যতম। স্বাধীনতা-উত্তর বাংলাদেশে ১৯৭২ সালের ৮ ফেব্রুয়ারি চিত্তরঞ্জন সাহা বর্তমান বাংলা একাডেমি প্রাঙ্গনের বটতলায় এক টুকরো চটের ওপর ৩২ খানা বই সাজিয়ে যে মেলার গোড়াপত্তন করেন তাতে বাংলা একাডেমি সম্পৃক্ত হয় ১৯৭৮ সালে। ১৯৮৩ সালে বাংলা একাডেমিতে অমর একুশে গ্রন্থমেলার প্রথম আয়োজন হয়। আজ বাংলা একাডেমি প্রাঙ্গণে স্থান সংকুলান না হওয়ায় ২০১৪ সাল থেকে বইমেলা সম্প্রসারিত করা হয় সোহরাওয়ার্দী উদ্যানে। পুরো ফেব্রুয়ারি মাস জুড়ে একুশে বইমেলাকে উপলক্ষ্য করে এই দেশের অগণিত ক্রেতা, দর্শক ও পাঠকের সমাবেশ ঘটে এবং একটা সাহিত্য-সাংস্কৃতিক পরিবেশের সৃষ্টি হয়। লেখক, সাহিত্যিক, সাংস্কৃতিককর্মী, রাজনৈতিক নেতাকর্মী, শিক্ষক, গবেষক, বুদ্ধিজীবী, সাংবাদিক-সহ নানা স্তরের মানুষের উপস্থিতিতে বইমেলা সরগরম হয়ে ওঠে। তবে তরুণ ছাত্রছাত্রীদের উপস্থিতিই বেশি। দূরদূরান্ত থেকে বহু প্রকাশক আসেন, আসে নানা ধরনের বই। অচেনা, অজানা অনেক বইয়ের সন্ধান মেলে। সব কেনা সম্ভব না হলেও অনেক বইয়ের সাথে পরিচয় ঘটে। প্রকাশক মেলা প্রাঙ্গণে ক্রেতাদের কাছে সরাসরি বই বিক্রির সুযোগ পান। সুযোগ পান পাঠকদের চাহিদা জানার। শত শত স্টলের মধ্য থেকে ক্রেতারা অভিরুচি অনুযায়ী স্টল নির্বাচন করে বই কিনতে পারেন। সীমাবদ্ধ প্রাঙ্গণে অসংখ্য রকম বইয়ের সমাবেশ ঘটায় ক্রেতারা স্বল্প আয়াসে পছন্দের বই সংগ্রহ করতে পারেন এবং প্রয়োজনীয় বইয়ের তালিকা সংগ্রহ করেন; যা পরবর্তীতে কাজে লাগে। একটি পাঠানুরাগী- প্রজন্ম গড়ে তুলতে এই মেলা অগ্রণী ভূমিকা পালন করে। মেলায় প্রতিদিন নতুন প্রকাশিত বই এসে যুক্ত হয়।  একুশের বইমেলায় পুরাতনের সাথে নতুন লেখকের মেলবন্ধন হয়। বহু নন্দিত কবি-সাহিত্যিক মেলায় এসে স্টলে বসেন, কৌতূহলী তরুণ বইপ্রেমিকদের সাথে কথা বলেন- অটোগ্রাফ দেন। লেখক-পাঠকের মহামিলন ঘটে এখানে। বইমেলার এক প্রান্তের মঞ্চে বিকেল-সন্ধ্যা মিলে চলে আলোচনা অনুষ্ঠান ও সাংস্কৃতিক অনুষ্ঠান যা দর্শকদের উপভোগ্য। অসংখ্য খাবার দোকানে চলে বন্ধুবান্ধবদের আড্ডা। তথ্যকেন্দ্র থেকে তথ্য দেওয়া হয় নতুন প্রকাশিত বইয়ের। মেলায় প্রবেশের সিংহতোরণটি থাকে দৃষ্টিনন্দিত ও সুসজ্জিত। একুশের বইমেলা আমাদের জাতীয় চেতনার দ্যোতক। এই মেলা আমাদের প্রাণ ও মনের মেলা। শত প্রাণের উদ্দীপ্ত উপস্থিতি, নতুন বইয়ের গন্ধে মৌ মৌ এই মেলা বাঙালির প্রেরণার উৎস। বইমেলা বই কেনা, পঠন-পাঠনের অভ্যাস ও ভাব বিনিময়ের মিলন তীর্থ।  যেকোনো বই মেলার গাম্ভীর্য, ভাব-ঐশ্বর্য ও ঐতিহ্য অক্ষুণ্ণ রাখার দায়িত্ব আমাদের সবার এবং এর মধ্য দিয়ে গড়ে উঠবে একটি শিক্ষিত ও সংস্কৃতিমনা বাঙালি জাতি।

cq_num: জাদুঘর
text:
জাদুঘর একটি শিক্ষামূলক ও সাংস্কৃতিক প্রতিষ্ঠান, যেখানে প্রাচীন নিদর্শন, ঐতিহাসিক দলিল, শিল্পকর্ম, প্রত্নতাত্ত্বিক সামগ্রী, ভাস্কর্য, হস্তশিল্প এবং বিভিন্ন যুগের সংগ্রহশালা সংরক্ষণ ও প্রদর্শনের জন্য রাখা হয়। এটি আমাদের অতীত ইতিহাস, ঐতিহ্য এবং সংস্কৃতি সম্পর্কে জানার একটি গুরুত্বপূর্ণ মাধ্যম। জাদুঘর হলো এমন এক জায়গা, যা আমাদের অতীতকে জানার জানালা খুলে দেয় এবং ইতিহাসের বিভিন্ন অধ্যায় সম্পর্কে সচেতন করে তোলে। একটি জাতির ঐতিহ্য ও সংস্কৃতির ধারক হিসেবে জাদুঘরের গুরুত্ব অপরিসীম। এখানে সংরক্ষিত নিদর্শনগুলো আমাদের জানান দেয় প্রাচীন কালের মানুষের জীবনযাত্রা, চিন্তাধারা, শিল্পকলা এবং সামাজিক রীতিনীতির কথা। জাদুঘরে ঘুরে আমরা বিভিন্ন যুগের মানুষের ব্যবহার্য জিনিসপত্র যেমন পোশাক, অস্ত্র, মুদ্রা, পাত্র, চিত্রকর্ম ইত্যাদি দেখতে পাই, যা আমাদের জ্ঞান ও দৃষ্টিভঙ্গিকে সমৃদ্ধ করে। জাদুঘর শুধু বিনোদনের স্থান নয়; এটি এক ধরনের জীবন্ত পাঠশালা। বিশেষ করে শিক্ষার্থীদের জন্য জাদুঘর একটি অনন্য জ্ঞানভান্ডার, যেখানে পাঠ্যবইয়ের বাইরের বাস্তব জগতের ইতিহাস সম্পর্কে জানার সুযোগ থাকে। এতে তারা ইতিহাস, বিজ্ঞান ও সংস্কৃতি সম্পর্কে বাস্তব অভিজ্ঞতা লাভ করে, যা তাদের শিক্ষা ও ব্যক্তিত্ব গঠনে সহায়ক ভূমিকা রাখে। গবেষকদের জন্যও জাদুঘর এক অমূল্য সম্পদ হিসেবে বিবেচিত হয়। বাংলাদেশে জাতীয় জাদুঘর ছাড়াও রয়েছে বিভিন্ন প্রত্নতত্ত্ব জাদুঘর, মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর, বিজ্ঞান
জাদুঘর এবং স্থানীয় ঐতিহ্যবাহী জাদুঘর, যেগুলো আমাদের মুক্তিযুদ্ধ, সাংস্কৃতিক উত্তরাধিকার এবং প্রাকৃতিক ঐতিহ্য সংরক্ষণে গুরুত্বপূর্ণ ভূমিকা রাখছে। সবশেষে বলা যায়, ইতিহাসচর্চা, জ্ঞানার্জন এবং জাতীয় চেতনাকে সমুন্নত রাখার জন্য জাদুঘরের গুরুত্ব অপরিসীম। আমাদের উচিত নিয়মিত জাদুঘর পরিদর্শন করা এবং ভবিষ্যৎ প্রজন্মকে এর সঙ্গে পরিচিত করে তোলা, যাতে তারা নিজেদের শিকড়ের সাথে দৃঢ়ভাবে যুক্ত থাকতে পারে এবং জাতীয় ঐতিহ্যকে সম্মান করতে শেখে।

অনুচ্ছেদ অন জাদুঘর জাদুঘর জাদুঘর জাদুঘর একটি শিক্ষামূলক ও সাংস্কৃতিক প্রতিষ্ঠান, যেখানে প্রাচীন নিদর্শন, ঐতিহাসিক দলিল, শিল্পকর্ম, প্রত্নতাত্ত্বিক সামগ্রী, ভাস্কর্য, হস্তশিল্প এবং বিভিন্ন যুগের সংগ্রহশালা সংরক্ষণ ও প্রদর্শনের জন্য রাখা হয়। এটি আমাদের অতীত ইতিহাস, ঐতিহ্য এবং সংস্কৃতি সম্পর্কে জানার একটি গুরুত্বপূর্ণ মাধ্যম। জাদুঘর হলো এমন এক জায়গা, যা আমাদের অতীতকে জানার জানালা খুলে দেয় এবং ইতিহাসের বিভিন্ন অধ্যায় সম্পর্কে সচেতন করে তোলে। একটি জাতির ঐতিহ্য ও সংস্কৃতির ধারক হিসেবে জাদুঘরের গুরুত্ব অপরিসীম। এখানে সংরক্ষিত নিদর্শনগুলো আমাদের জানান দেয় প্রাচীন কালের মানুষের জীবনযাত্রা, চিন্তাধারা, শিল্পকলা এবং সামাজিক রীতিনীতির কথা। জাদুঘরে ঘুরে আমরা বিভিন্ন যুগের মানুষের ব্যবহার্য জিনিসপত্র যেমন পোশাক, অস্ত্র, মুদ্রা, পাত্র, চিত্রকর্ম ইত্যাদি দেখতে পাই, যা আমাদের জ্ঞান ও দৃষ্টিভঙ্গিকে সমৃদ্ধ করে। জাদুঘর শুধু বিনোদনের স্থান নয়; এটি এক ধরনের জীবন্ত পাঠশালা। বিশেষ করে শিক্ষার্থীদের জন্য জাদুঘর একটি অনন্য জ্ঞানভান্ডার, যেখানে পাঠ্যবইয়ের বাইরের বাস্তব জগতের ইতিহাস সম্পর্কে জানার সুযোগ থাকে। এতে তারা ইতিহাস, বিজ্ঞান ও সংস্কৃতি সম্পর্কে বাস্তব অভিজ্ঞতা লাভ করে, যা তাদের শিক্ষা ও ব্যক্তিত্ব গঠনে সহায়ক ভূমিকা রাখে। গবেষকদের জন্যও জাদুঘর এক অমূল্য সম্পদ হিসেবে বিবেচিত হয়। বাংলাদেশে জাতীয় জাদুঘর ছাড়াও রয়েছে বিভিন্ন প্রত্নতত্ত্ব জাদুঘর, মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর, বিজ্ঞান
জাদুঘর এবং স্থানীয় ঐতিহ্যবাহী জাদুঘর, যেগুলো আমাদের মুক্তিযুদ্ধ, সাংস্কৃতিক উত্তরাধিকার এবং প্রাকৃতিক ঐতিহ্য সংরক্ষণে গুরুত্বপূর্ণ ভূমিকা রাখছে। সবশেষে বলা যায়, ইতিহাসচর্চা, জ্ঞানার্জন এবং জাতীয় চেতনাকে সমুন্নত রাখার জন্য জাদুঘরের গুরুত্ব অপরিসীম। আমাদের উচিত নিয়মিত জাদুঘর পরিদর্শন করা এবং ভবিষ্যৎ প্রজন্মকে এর সঙ্গে পরিচিত করে তোলা, যাতে তারা নিজেদের শিকড়ের সাথে দৃঢ়ভাবে যুক্ত থাকতে পারে এবং জাতীয় ঐতিহ্যকে সম্মান করতে শেখে।

cq_num: মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর
text: বাংলাদেশের স্বাধীনতা অর্জনের গৌরবোজ্জ্বল ইতিহাস ধারণ করে আছে মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর। ১৯৭১ সালের মুক্তিযুদ্ধ আমাদের জাতির অস্তিত্ব রক্ষার লড়াই। এই যুদ্ধের প্রতিটি মুহূর্ত, ত্যাগ, বীরত্ব এবং সংগ্রামের সাক্ষী হয়ে আছে এই জাদুঘর। ১৯৯৬ সালের ২২ মার্চ ঢাকার সেগুনবাগিচায় মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘরের যাত্রা শুরু হয়। বর্তমানে এটি একটি পূর্ণাঙ্গ প্রতিষ্ঠান হিসেবে গণমানুষের চেতনা জাগ্রত করার কাজে নিয়োজিত। জাদুঘরে প্রবেশ করলে চোখে পড়ে শহীদ মুক্তিযোদ্ধাদের ব্যবহৃত অস্ত্রশস্ত্র, পোশাক, মানচিত্র, দলিলপত্র, সংবাদপত্রের কাটিং, ছবি ও মুক্তিযুদ্ধকালীন নানা উপকরণ। এখানে প্রদর্শিত হচ্ছে গণহত্যায় ব্যবহৃত পাকিস্তানি সেনাদের সরঞ্জাম এবং তাদের নৃশংসতার সাক্ষ্য বহনকারী অনেক প্রমাণ। এ ছাড়া মুক্তিযুদ্ধ চলাকালীন শরণার্থী শিবিরের ছবি, গণহত্যার নিদর্শন এবং আন্তর্জাতিক গণমাধ্যমে প্রকাশিত প্রতিবেদনও সংরক্ষিত রয়েছে। মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘরের আরেকটি উল্লেখযোগ্য দিক হলো মুক্তিযোদ্ধা ও শহীদ পরিবারের জীবন্ত সাক্ষ্য। এখানে আসলে দর্শনার্থীরা শুধু ইতিহাসের তথ্যই পান না, বরং সেই সময়কার বাস্তব কষ্ট ও সংগ্রামের গভীর অনুভূতি উপলব্ধি করতে পারেন। বিশেষভাবে তৈরি গ্যালারি ও প্রদর্শনী কক্ষগুলোতে বাংলাদেশের স্বাধীনতার জন্য দেওয়া অকৃত্রিম আত্মত্যাগের দৃশ্য ফুটে ওঠে। এই জাদুঘর শুধু ইতিহাস সংরক্ষণের স্থান নয়, এটি আগামী প্রজন্মের কাছে মুক্তিযুদ্ধের চেতনা পৌঁছে দেওয়ার একটি মহৎ মাধ্যম। তরুণ সমাজ এখানে এসে জানতে পারে স্বাধীনতা কিভাবে এসেছে, কাদের রক্তের বিনিময়ে আজকের বাংলাদেশ গড়ে উঠেছে। মুক্তিযুদ্ধের সত্য ইতিহাস জানলে তরুণরা দেশপ্রেমে উদ্বুদ্ধ হয় এবং জাতি গঠনে অনুপ্রাণিত হয়। সর্বোপরি, মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর আমাদের জাতীয় ঐতিহ্যের এক উজ্জ্বল প্রতীক। এটি আমাদের শেখায় যে স্বাধীনতা কোনো কাকতালীয় ঘটনা নয়, বরং দীর্ঘ সংগ্রাম ও আত্মত্যাগের ফসল। তাই প্রত্যেক নাগরিকের উচিত মুক্তিযুদ্ধ জাদুঘর পরিদর্শন করা এবং স্বাধীনতার ইতিহাস সম্পর্কে সচেতন হওয়া। এই জাদুঘর আমাদের মুক্তির গৌরবময় কাহিনীকে চিরকাল জীবন্ত করে রাখবে।

cq_num: কম্পিউটার
text:
আধুনিক যুগে কম্পিউটার মানবজীবনের অবিচ্ছেদ্য অংশ হয়ে দাঁড়িয়েছে। এটি একটি অসাধারণ আবিষ্কার, যা মানুষের দৈনন্দিন কাজ থেকে শুরু করে জটিল বৈজ্ঞানিক গবেষণায় অপরিসীম ভূমিকা রাখছে। ‘কম্পিউটার’ শব্দের অর্থ হলো গণনাকারী যন্ত্র। প্রথমদিকে এটি মূলত হিসাব-নিকাশের জন্য ব্যবহৃত হতো। কিন্তু প্রযুক্তির উন্নতির সাথে সাথে আজ কম্পিউটার তথ্যপ্রযুক্তির প্রধান মাধ্যম হিসেবে আমাদের জীবনের প্রতিটি ক্ষেত্রে গভীরভাবে প্রভাব বিস্তার করেছে।
কম্পিউটার দিয়ে মুহূর্তের মধ্যে বিশাল পরিমাণ তথ্য সংরক্ষণ, প্রক্রিয়াজাত ও বিশ্লেষণ করা যায়। শিক্ষা ক্ষেত্রে কম্পিউটার ব্যবহার করে শিক্ষার্থীরা অনলাইনে পড়াশোনা করতে পারে, বিশ্বজুড়ে বিভিন্ন জ্ঞান আহরণ করতে পারে এবং নিজেদের জ্ঞানকে সমৃদ্ধ করতে পারে। চিকিৎসা বিজ্ঞানে কম্পিউটার রোগ নির্ণয়, অস্ত্রোপচার পরিচালনা, গবেষণা ও ঔষধ আবিষ্কারে অত্যন্ত কার্যকর ভূমিকা রাখছে। ব্যবসায়িক ক্ষেত্রে হিসাব-নিকাশ, অনলাইন বাণিজ্য, ব্যাংকিং কার্যক্রম ইত্যাদি সহজ ও দ্রুত করার জন্য কম্পিউটার অপরিহার্য হয়ে উঠেছে। এছাড়াও বিনোদন জগতে কম্পিউটার গুরুত্বপূর্ণ ভূমিকা রাখছে। গেমস, মুভি, গান, অ্যানিমেশন ও সামাজিক যোগাযোগ মাধ্যম সবই কম্পিউটার প্রযুক্তির অবদান। যোগাযোগ ব্যবস্থায় ই-মেইল, ভিডিও কল, মেসেজিং সেবা ইত্যাদির মাধ্যমে মানুষ পৃথিবীর এক প্রান্ত থেকে অন্য প্রান্তে মুহূর্তেই যোগাযোগ করতে পারছে। সরকারি-বেসরকারি প্রতিষ্ঠান, সামরিক বাহিনী, আবহাওয়ার পূর্বাভাস, মহাকাশ গবেষণা—সব ক্ষেত্রেই কম্পিউটার অপরিহার্য। তবে কম্পিউটারের কিছু নেতিবাচক দিকও রয়েছে। অতিরিক্ত ব্যবহার চোখের ক্ষতি, শারীরিক অসুস্থতা এবং মানসিক ক্লান্তি ডেকে আনে। এছাড়া অনলাইন অপরাধ, তথ্য চুরি এবং আসক্তির মতো সমস্যাও বাড়ছে। তাই কম্পিউটার ব্যবহারের ক্ষেত্রে সচেতনতা ও সঠিক দিকনির্দেশনা জরুরি। সর্বোপরি বলা যায়, কম্পিউটার হলো আধুনিক বিজ্ঞানের এক অনন্য দান। এটি মানুষের জীবনকে সহজ, দ্রুত ও আরামদায়ক করেছে। সঠিকভাবে ব্যবহার করলে কম্পিউটার আমাদের দেশ ও জাতিকে উন্নতির শিখরে পৌঁছে দিতে পারে। তাই প্রত্যেকের উচিত কম্পিউটার জ্ঞান অর্জন করা এবং একে কল্যাণমূলক কাজে ব্যবহার করা।
